jobs.title: Private Banking Business Manager (Contract)
jobs.description:
## Main Duties

- Coordinate and facilitate trainings (system, process, policies/procedures) for Front Office and business support staff, ensuring all team members are up to date with the latest systems, operational processes, and policy/regulatory requirements. Maintain accurate attendance records and follow up with staff to ensure mandatory training is completed within required timeframes. Prepare and submit monthly training completion reports to PBCOO and HR.

- Manage and oversee onboarding training for new Front Office hires by tracking their completion of mandatory trainings. Liaise with relevant departments to ensure timely granting of system access rights upon successful completion of onboarding requirements, supporting a smooth transition into their roles.

- Organize monthly CRO/CSO meetings and collaborate with Department Heads to schedule and coordinate monthly meetings for Front Office. Prepare meeting materials/training deck and ensure that Front Office staff are informed of any updates or changes to policies, procedures, regulations, or systems that impact Private Banking operations.

- Partner with Market Group Heads on CRO/CSO cost allocation reporting to collect, review, and consolidate data for monthly CRO/CSO cost allocation reports. Ensure accuracy and timely submission of reports, supporting effective financial management and transparency within the Private Banking division.

- Handle and respond to ad-hoc requests for customized statements, reference letters, and perform reviews of operational incidents. Ensure all requests from Front Office are handled efficiently, accurately, and in accordance with internal policies and regulatory requirements.

- Provide prompt and professional support to Front Office staff and serve as a primary contact point for non-KYC related queries received via the PBS group mailbox. Deliver high-quality, timely support to enable Front Office staff to operate efficiently and effectively.

- Perform ex-ante compliance reviews of deposits initiated by Front Office staff, verifying compliance with regulatory requirements and internal guidelines. Ensure all checks are completed before daily cut-off times to mitigate operational and compliance risks.

- Participate in and contribute to special assignments, process improvement initiatives, and system enhancement projects as designated by Management. Where the PBCOO function serves as process owner, leads and manage change initiatives to optimize workflows and enhance system functionality.

- Support User Acceptance Testing (UAT) for system changes and participate in UAT activities for new system implementations or enhancements. Organize the nomination of Front Office testers, provide guidance on testing procedures, and ensure thorough documentation and feedback to support successful system rollouts.

- Manage and oversee a range of administrative duties, such as distributing faxes, reassigning name screening tasks, and translating documents as needed. Ensure all administrative processes are handled efficiently to support the smooth operation of Private Banking activities.

- Monitor and manage the Client Enquiry and Feedback mailbox, ensuring all general client inquiries are acknowledged and responded to promptly and professionally. Escalate complex issues as necessary and maintain high standards of client service.

- Conduct data analysis and compile management information system (MIS) reports to support management reporting requirements. Provide analytical insights and data support to various risk and governance committees, facilitating informed decision-making within the Private Banking division.

- Support corporate governance initiatives and assist in the implementation and monitoring of corporate governance frameworks and practices within Private Banking. Ensure adherence to internal policies, regulatory requirements, and industry best practices. Contribute to the preparation of materials for governance committees and facilitate communication between stakeholders to promote accountability and transparency.

## Requirements

- University degree with a minimum of 8 years’ experience in financial services, preferable within Private Banking. Keen interest in the financial industry and wealth management.

- Strong understanding of relevant regulatory and compliance frameworks and comprehensive knowledge of banking operations

- Excellent command of both written and spoken English and Chinese (Cantonese & Putonghua)

- Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications, including Outlook, Word, PowerPoint, and Excel.

- Experience in coordinating cross-functional projects or initiatives within a financial institution is an advantage, including process improvement, change management, or system implementation projects

- Collaborative team player with the ability to work independently in a fast-paced environment.

- Able to perform well under pressure and possess strong organizational skills including ability to meet tight deadlines, manage multiple priorities concurrently and adapt to changing business needs.

- Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to think out-of-the-box and develop innovative solutions. Proactive, highly self-motivated and result-oriented individual with attention to detail.

- Eager-to-learn with a service-oriented mindset and committed to delivering high-quality service

jobs.title: Senior Credit Advisory and Credit Structuring Specialist
jobs.description:
The hire will be responsible for managing the structured lending transactions from origination to closing.

## Main responsibilities include

1) Complex or non-standard credit transactions:
- Lead, design and structure solutions for complex or non-standard credit transactions as per agreed Target Operating Model for Credit Solutions. Establish and maintain strong internal relationship with diverse stakeholders to grow the loan book.
- Act as First Line of defence by providing advice and guidance to CROs on credit transactions as well as lead discussions with the Credit Risk Approval Team for assessment and approval.
- Provide support to CROs in prospect and client calls and visits in discussing credit terms.
- Underwrite credit memo which includes presenting the transaction to EFG’s Credit Committees to obtain the necessary approval.
- Discuss, negotiate and review the credit terms with Credit Approval and Legal Teams to ensure the terms and conditions are represented in the Finance Documents.

2) Credit related product management:
- Responsible for delivering the KPIs relating to Structured Lending in Hong Kong
- Engage and discuss with CROs and Market Group / Business Heads to pro-actively identify credit related business opportunities.
- Takes the lead and ownership for the development of new credit related product initiatives.
- Engage with the Regional Business Head to formulate and adapt the Credit product strategy for the HK Branch and monitor execution.

3) Credit process:
- Ensure compliance to credit and control procedures and adherence to the EFG’s risk appetite guidance.
- Able to anticipate and provide feasible solutions to complete the credit transaction. Provide guidance to CROs / MGHs on matters relating to credit transactions.
- Assist with reporting and monitoring of covenants for specific key transactions.
- As and when required, participate in the interactions between Origination, the Credit Team, and other stakeholders on credit related initiatives.
- Contributes to the development of tools and new processes that will lead to a higher degree of efficiency in the credit value chain.
- As and when required, provides input on credit policies / risk methodologies and directives whereby Credit Office is the owner.

Qualifications

- Holds a relevant Bachelor or Master’s degree in Business, Finance or comparable degree.
- Relevant professional accreditations will be advantageous.
- More than 10 years of strong credit management experience in a Private Bank or a Commercial Bank /Investment Bank.

Preferred Skills

- Expert knowledge of structured lending including single stock, illiquid assets and mortgage lending.
- Well versed in financial services products and local regulatory requirements
- Experience with underwriting other asset classes.
- Strong interpersonal and communication skills to work in a dynamic environment
- Strong ability to communicate, read and write in English fluently
- Proficiency in Business Mandarin is preferred.

- Act as First Line of defence by providing advice and guidance to CROs on credit transactions as well as lead discussions with the Credit Risk Approval Team for assessment and approval.
- Provide support to CROs in prospect and client calls and visits in discussing credit terms.
- Underwrite credit memo which includes presenting the transaction to EFG’s Credit Committees to obtain the necessary approval.
- Discuss, negotiate and review the credit terms with Credit Approval and Legal Teams to ensure the terms and conditions are represented in the Finance Documents.

2) Credit related product management:
- Responsible for delivering the KPIs relating to Structured Lending in Hong Kong
- Engage and discuss with CROs and Market Group / Business Heads to pro-actively identify credit related business opportunities.
- Takes the lead and ownership for the development of new credit related product initiatives.
- Engage with the Regional Business Head to formulate and adapt the Credit product strategy for the HK Branch and monitor execution.

3) Credit process:
- Ensure compliance to credit and control procedures and adherence to the EFG’s risk appetite guidance.
- Able to anticipate and provide feasible solutions to complete the credit transaction. Provide guidance to CROs / MGHs on matters relating to credit transactions.
- Assist with reporting and monitoring of covenants for specific key transactions.
- As and when required, participate in the interactions between Origination, the Credit Team, and other stakeholders on credit related initiatives.
- Contributes to the development of tools and new processes that will lead to a higher degree of efficiency in the credit value chain.
- As and when required, provides input on credit policies / risk methodologies and directives whereby Credit Office is the owner.

Qualifications

- Holds a relevant Bachelor or Master’s degree in Business, Finance or comparable degree.
- Relevant professional accreditations will be advantageous.
- More than 10 years of strong credit management experience in a Private Bank or a Commercial Bank /Investment Bank.

Preferred Skills

- Expert knowledge of structured lending including single stock, illiquid assets and mortgage lending.
- Well versed in financial services products and local regulatory requirements
- Experience with underwriting other asset classes.
- Strong interpersonal and communication skills to work in a dynamic environment
- Strong ability to communicate, read and write in English fluently
- Proficiency in Business Mandarin is preferred.

jobs.title: Intern Credit Risk
jobs.description:
## Our Purpose and Mission

Empowering entrepreneurial minds to create value – today and for the future.

We are a private bank, offering personalised solutions on a global scale to private and institutional clients. Our sustainable success is based on our talents and on how we partner with our clients and communities to create lasting value.

## Your Main Responsibilities

Under the supervision of the Credit Team, you will:

- Support Credit Officers to evaluate, analyze and structure credit requests (primarily Lombard facilities incl. FX/derivative business, EU mortgage loans (RRE) under MCD and other loans/credit cards) within delegated competencies booked in Luxembourg.

- Draft credit documentation e.g. credit contracts & pledge agreements etc.

- Follow-up of missing credit documentation

- Support the daily shortfall monitoring and take appropriate follow-up actions.

- Ensure the proper administration and maintenance of the credit portfolio and credit files.

Skills and experience

- Bachelor's degree (minimum) in Finance, Business Administration, Risk Management, or a related field.

- You are familiar with working in an international environment. English is a prerequisite. French is considered a strong asset;

- You are proficient with Excel and the Office Suite;

- You have strong organisational and analytical skills.

- Knowledge of credit risk management principles a plus

- Accountability: Taking ownership for tasks and challenges, as well as seeking continuous improvement

- Hands-on: Being proactive to rapidly deliver high-quality results

- Passionate: Being committed and striving for excellence

- Solution-driven: Focusing on client outcomes and treating clients fairly with a risk-aware mindset

- Partnership-oriented: Promoting collaboration and teamwork. Working together with an entrepreneurial spirit.

- Please ensure to attach a cover letter to your CV when filling the application.

Our Values

- Accountability: Taking ownership for tasks and challenges, as well as seeking continuous improvement

- Hands-on: Being proactive to rapidly deliver high-quality results

- Passionate: Being committed and striving for excellence

- Solution-driven: Focusing on client outcomes and treating clients fairly with a risk-aware mindset

- Partnership-oriented: Promoting collaboration and teamwork. Working together with an entrepreneurial spirit.

- Please ensure to attach a cover letter to your CV when filling the application.

Il nostro scopo e la nostra missione che alimentano le menti imprenditoriali per creare valore – oggi e per il futuro. Siamo una banca privata, offrendo soluzioni personalizzate su scala globale a clienti privati e istituzionali. Il nostro successo sostenibile si basa sui nostri talenti e su come collaboriamo con i nostri clienti e comunità per creare valore duraturo.

## Le tue principali responsabilità Sotto la supervisione del Credit Team, Lei:
- Supporta i Credit Officers per valutare, analizzare e strutturare le richieste di credito (principalmente le strutture lombarda incl. FX/derivative business, mutui ipotecari UE (RRE) sotto MCD e altri prestiti / carte di credito) all'interno delle competenze delegati prenotate in Lussemburgo.
- Progetto di documentazione di credito, ad esempio contratti di credito e contratti di impegno, ecc.
- Seguire la documentazione di credito mancante
- Sostenere il monitoraggio giornaliero delle carenze e intraprendere azioni di follow-up appropriate.
- Assicurare la corretta amministrazione e manutenzione del portafoglio di credito e dei file di credito. Competenze ed esperienza
- Laurea (minimo) in Finanza, Business Administration, Risk Management, o un campo relativo.
- No. Conoscete il lavoro in un ambiente internazionale. L'inglese è un prerequisito. Il francese è considerato un bene forte;
- Sei abile con Excel e la Suite Office;
- Hai forti capacità organizzative e analitiche.
- Conoscenza dei principi di gestione del rischio di credito un plus
- Accountability: Assumere la proprietà per i compiti e le sfide, così come la ricerca di un miglioramento continuo
- Hands-on: Essere proattivi a fornire rapidamente risultati di alta qualità
- Passionate: Essere impegnati e impegnati per l'eccellenza
- Soluzione-driven: Focusing sui risultati del cliente e trattare i clienti abbastanza con una mentalità di rischio-aware
- Partnership-oriented: Promuovere la collaborazione e il lavoro di squadra. Lavorare insieme a uno spirito imprenditoriale.
- Assicurarsi di allegare una lettera di copertura al CV durante la compilazione dell'applicazione. I nostri valori
- Responsabilità: Prendere la proprietà per compiti e sfide, così come la ricerca di un miglioramento continuo

- Passionate: Essere impegnati e impegnati per l'eccellenza
- Solution-driven: Focusing sui risultati del cliente e trattare i clienti abbastanza con una mentalità di rischio-consapevole
- Partnership-oriented: Promuovere la collaborazione e il lavoro di squadra. Lavorare insieme a uno spirito imprenditoriale.
- Assicurarsi di allegare una lettera di copertura al CV durante la compilazione dell'applicazione.

Unsere Aufgabe und Mission, Unternehmergeist zu stärken, um Wert zu schaffen – heute und für die Zukunft. Wir sind eine private Bank und bieten personalisierte Lösungen weltweit an private und institutionelle Kunden. Unser nachhaltiger Erfolg basiert auf unseren Talenten und darauf, wie wir mit unseren Kunden und Gemeinschaften zusammenarbeiten, um dauerhaften Wert zu schaffen. Ihre wichtigsten Aufgaben Unter der Aufsicht des Credit Teams unterstützen Sie die Credit Officers, Kreditanfragen zu evaluieren, zu analysieren und zu strukturieren (vor allem Lombard Anlagen inkl. FX/Derivategeschäft, EU-Kreditdarlehen (RRE) unter MCD und andere Kredite/Kreditkarten) innerhalb delegierter in Luxemburg gebuchter Kompetenzen.
- Entwurf einer Kreditdokumentation, z.B. Kreditverträge & Pfandverträge usw.
- Nachverfolgung fehlender Kreditdokumentation
- Unterstützung der täglichen Fehlerüberwachung und Durchführung entsprechender Folgemaßnahmen.
- Stellen Sie die ordnungsgemäße Verwaltung und Wartung des Kreditportfolios und der Kreditdateien sicher. Fähigkeiten und Erfahrung
- Bachelor-Abschluss (Mindeststudium) in Finanzen, Betriebswirtschaft, Risikomanagement oder einem verwandten Bereich.
- Ja. Sie kennen die Arbeit in einem internationalen Umfeld. Englisch ist eine Voraussetzung. Französisch gilt als ein starkes Gut;
- Sie sind mit Excel und der Office Suite vertraut;
- Sie haben starke organisatorische und analytische Fähigkeiten.
- Wissen über Kreditrisikomanagementprinzipien ein Plus
- Rechenschaftspflicht: Eigentumsbesitz für Aufgaben und Herausforderungen zu nehmen, sowie kontinuierliche Verbesserung zu suchen
- Hands-on: Being proaktiv, um schnell qualitativ hochwertige Ergebnisse zu liefern
- Leidenschaft: Engagement und Streben nach Exzellenz
- Lösungsgetrieben: Fokussieren auf Kundenergebnisse und Behandlung von Kunden fair mit einem risikobewussten Mindset - partnerschaftliche Förderung der Zusammenarbeit und Teamarbeit. Zusammenarbeit mit einem Unternehmergeist.
- Bitte stellen Sie sicher, dass Sie beim Ausfüllen der Bewerbung einen Brief an Ihren Lebenslauf anbringen. Unsere Werte

- Hands-on: Being proaktiv, um schnell qualitativ hochwertige Ergebnisse zu liefern
- Passionate: Engagement und Streben nach Exzellenz

Notre réussite durable repose sur nos talents et sur la façon dont nous collaborons avec nos clients et nos collectivités pour créer une valeur durable. Vos principales responsabilités Sous la supervision de l'équipe de crédit, vous devrez : - soutenir les agents de crédit pour évaluer, analyser et structurer les demandes de crédit (principalement les installations lombardes y compris. FX/activité dérivée, prêts hypothécaires de l'UE (RRE) dans le cadre du MCD et autres prêts/cartes de crédit) dans les limites des compétences déléguées enregistrées au Luxembourg.

- Baccalauréat (minimum) en finance, administration des affaires, gestion des risques ou dans un domaine connexe.

- Veuillez joindre une lettre d'accompagnement à votre CV au moment de remplir la demande. Nos valeurs
- Responsabilisation : prise en charge des tâches et des défis, et recherche d'une amélioration continue
- Pratique : être proactif pour obtenir rapidement des résultats de grande qualité
- Passionné : être engagé et viser l'excellence
- Inspiré par la solution : se concentrer sur les résultats des clients et traiter les clients avec équité et sensibilisation aux risques
- Objectif du partenariat : promouvoir la collaboration et le travail d'équipe. Travailler ensemble avec un esprit d'entreprise.
- Veuillez joindre une lettre d'accompagnement à votre CV au moment de remplir la demande.

jobs.title: Information Security Risk Officer
jobs.description:
## 1. ICT Risk Management & Regulatory Compliance

- Contribute to the annual ICT Risk Framework report by gathering data and drafting sections for CISO review

- Track regulatory developments (DORA, local circulars) and prepare impact assessments

- Maintain compliance documentation and support regulatory reporting activities

- Assist in preparing materials for regulator communications and audits

 2.    Third Party Risk Management (TPRM)

- Perform security due diligence and risk assessments on new and existing ICT service providers

- Monitor third-party compliance with contractual security requirements and SLAs

- Follow up on third-party security incidents and escalate as required

 3.    Major Incident Management & Regulatory Reporting

- Execute incident response procedures and participate in security incident investigations

- Assist in classifying incidents according to DORA major incident criteria

- Draft regulatory major incident notifications (initial, intermediate, final reports) for CISO validation

- Maintain incident logs and support aggregated annual cost/loss reporting

- Perform post-incident reviews and track remediation actions to completion

 4.    Business Continuity Management (BCM) & Operational Resilience

- Assist in developing and maintaining IT Business Continuity Plans (BCP)

- Participate in BCP testing activities and document test results

- Support digital operational resilience testing activities when applicable

- Maintain Business Impact Analyses (BIA) for critical ICT systems and services

  5. Security Operations & Monitoring

- Perform daily security monitoring, including data leakage prevention alerts, and business as usual activities

- Monitor vulnerability assessments and track remediation of identified findings

- Maintain security documentation including policies, procedures, and technical standards

- Participate in security architecture reviews and project security assessments

  6. Governance & Security Awareness

- Follow-up the Information Security Awareness programs including phishing simulations, annual class-rooms awareness trainings…

- Prepare security reports and KRIs.

- Perform security reviews of new projects, systems, and cloud deployments

Skills and experience

- Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in IT, Cybersecurity, or related field

- Minimum 5 years of experience in Information

- Experience in financial services/banking sector

- Good knowledge of security frameworks (ISO 27001, NIST CSF)

- Familiarity with DORA, GDPR, CSSF requirements

- Experience with vulnerability scanning and SIEM tools

- Understanding of BCM practices

- Experience with third-party security assessments

- Basic knowledge of cloud security (Azure, AWS)

- Fluent in English and French

Our Values

Accountability: Taking ownership for tasks and challenges, as well as seeking continuous improvement

Hands-on: Being proactive to rapidly deliver high-quality results

Passionate: Being committed and striving for excellence

Solution-driven: Focusing on client outcomes and treating clients fairly with a risk-aware mindset

Partnership-oriented: Promoting collaboration and teamwork. Working together with an entrepreneurial spirit.

 2.    Third Party Risk Management (TPRM)

- Perform security due diligence and risk assessments on new and existing ICT service providers

- Monitor third-party compliance with contractual security requirements and SLAs

- Follow up on third-party security incidents and escalate as required

 3.    Major Incident Management & Regulatory Reporting

- Execute incident response procedures and participate in security incident investigations

- Assist in classifying incidents according to DORA major incident criteria

- Draft regulatory major incident notifications (initial, intermediate, final reports) for CISO validation

- Maintain incident logs and support aggregated annual cost/loss reporting

- Perform post-incident reviews and track remediation actions to completion

 4.    Business Continuity Management (BCM) & Operational Resilience

- Assist in developing and maintaining IT Business Continuity Plans (BCP)

- Participate in BCP testing activities and document test results

- Support digital operational resilience testing activities when applicable

- Maintain Business Impact Analyses (BIA) for critical ICT systems and services

  5. Security Operations & Monitoring

- Perform daily security monitoring, including data leakage prevention alerts, and business as usual activities

- Monitor vulnerability assessments and track remediation of identified findings

- Maintain security documentation including policies, procedures, and technical standards

- Participate in security architecture reviews and project security assessments

  6. Governance & Security Awareness

- Follow-up the Information Security Awareness programs including phishing simulations, annual class-rooms awareness trainings…

- Prepare security reports and KRIs.

- Perform security reviews of new projects, systems, and cloud deployments

Skills and experience

- Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in IT, Cybersecurity, or related field

- Minimum 5 years of experience in Information

- Experience in financial services/banking sector

- Good knowledge of security frameworks (ISO 27001, NIST CSF)

- Familiarity with DORA, GDPR, CSSF requirements

- Experience with vulnerability scanning and SIEM tools

- Understanding of BCM practices

- Experience with third-party security assessments

- Basic knowledge of cloud security (Azure, AWS)

- Fluent in English and French

Our Values

Accountability: Taking ownership for tasks and challenges, as well as seeking continuous improvement

Hands-on: Being proactive to rapidly deliver high-quality results

Passionate: Being committed and striving for excellence

Solution-driven: Focusing on client outcomes and treating clients fairly with a risk-aware mindset

Partnership-oriented: Promoting collaboration and teamwork. Working together with an entrepreneurial spirit.

descriptionByLocale.fr:
1. Gestion des risques liés aux TIC et conformité à la réglementation
- Contribuer au rapport annuel sur le Cadre de gestion des risques liés aux TIC en recueillant des données et en rédigeant des sections pour l'examen de l'ISO
- Suivre les développements réglementaires (DORA, circulaires locales) et préparer des évaluations d'impact
- Maintenir la documentation sur la conformité et appuyer les activités de déclaration réglementaire
- Aider à préparer des documents pour les communications et les vérifications des organismes de réglementation 2. Gestion des risques des tiers
- Effectuer des évaluations des risques et de la diligence raisonnable en matière de sécurité à l'égard des fournisseurs de services TIC nouveaux et existants
- Surveiller la conformité des tiers aux exigences contractuelles en matière de sécurité et aux ALS
- Suivi des incidents de sécurité des tiers et escalader au besoin 3. Gestion des incidents majeurs et rapports réglementaires
- Exécuter les procédures d'intervention en cas d'incident et participer aux enquêtes sur les incidents de sécurité
- Aider à classer les incidents selon les critères d'incident majeur de la DORA
- Ébauche des notifications réglementaires d'incidents majeurs (rapports initiaux, intermédiaires et finaux) pour la validation de l'ISO
- Tenir des registres des incidents et appuyer la déclaration annuelle agrégée des coûts et des pertes
- Effectuer des examens après les incidents et suivre les mesures d'assainissement jusqu'à leur achèvement 4. Gestion de la continuité des activités et résilience opérationnelle
- Aider à élaborer et à maintenir des plans de continuité des activités en TI (PCA)
- Participer aux activités d'essai du PCA et documenter les résultats d'essai
- Soutenir les activités d'essai de résilience opérationnelle numérique, s'il y a lieu
- Maintenir les analyses d'impact opérationnel (EIV) pour les systèmes et services TIC essentiels 5. Opérations et surveillance de la sécurité
- Effectuer une surveillance quotidienne de la sécurité, y compris les alertes de prévention des fuites de données, et les activités courantes
- Surveiller les évaluations de la vulnérabilité et suivre l'assainissement des constatations identifiées
- Maintenir la documentation sur la sécurité, y compris les politiques, les procédures et les normes techniques
- Participer aux examens de l'architecture de sécurité et aux évaluations de la sécurité des projets 6.
- Examen de la sécurité des nouveaux projets, systèmes et déploiements dans le cloud Compétences et expérience
- Baccalauréat ou maîtrise en TI, cybersécurité ou domaine connexe
- Au moins 5 ans d'expérience en information
- Expérience en services financiers/secteur bancaire
- Bonne connaissance des cadres de sécurité (ISO 27001, CSF NIST)
- Connaissance des exigences de la DORA, du RGPD, de la CSSF
- Expérience de l'analyse de la vulnérabilité et des outils SIEM
- Compréhension des pratiques de la BCM
- Expérience de l'évaluation de la sécurité par des tiers
- Connaissance de la sécurité dans le cloud (Azure, AWS)
- Fluent en français et en anglais Nos valeurs Responsabilité : S'approprier les tâches et les défis, et chercher une amélioration continue Hands-on: Être proactif pour obtenir rapidement des résultats de haute qualité Passionné: S'engager et s'efforcer d'atteindre l'excellence Inspiré par la solution: Se concentrer sur les résultats des clients et traiter les clients équitablement avec un état d'esprit conscient des risques. Travailler ensemble avec un esprit d'entreprise.

jobs.title: Head of Structured Solutions - Asia
jobs.description:
## Main responsibilities

- Leading the Structured Products Distribution & Offering in Asia

- Partner with the Client Relationship Officers and Investment Counsellors to increase Structured Products revenues and penetration

- Drive innovation in structured solutions offering

- Interact with local Investment Banks and monitor market & product trends

- Ensure compliance with regulatory requirements in all structured solutions activities

- Build and maintain strong relationships with key stakeholders (Global Markets, Product Due Diligence, Risk & Compliance, Operations…)

- Foster a culture of continuous learning and development within the Client Facing Employee community

- Support the IT projects to build a scalable structured solutions platform

- Identify and mitigate risks associated with structured products projects

Skills and Experience

- Approximately 10 years of experience in structured solutions sales management

- Strong technical expertise in flow and complex structured solutions

- Proven track record of successful distribution strategy development and implementation.

- Fluent in English, proficiency in an additional Asian language is a plus

- Strong leadership and communication skills.

- Entrepreneur attitude and independent contributor to a dynamic regional & functional environment

- Result oriented with strong attention to details.

## Our Values

- Accountability : Taking ownership for tasks and challenges, as well as seeking continuous improvement

- Hands-on : Being proactive to rapidly deliver high-quality results

- Passionate : Being committed and striving for excellence

- Solution-driven : Focusing on client outcomes and treating clients fairly with a risk-aware mindset

- Partnership-oriented : Promoting collaboration and teamwork. Working together with an entrepreneurial spirit.

sections.heading: Our Company
paragraphs: EFG International is a global private banking group, offering private banking and asset management services. We serve clients in over 40 locations worldwide. EFG International offers a stimulating and dynamic work environment and strives to be an employer of choice.

EFG is committed to providing an equitable and inclusive working environment that is founded on the principle of mutual respect. Joining our team means experiencing a supportive environment, where your contributions are valued and recognised. We strongly believe that the diversity of our teams gives us a competitive advantage by fostering better decision-making and greater innovation.

sections.heading: Our Purpose and Mission
paragraphs: Empowering entrepreneurial minds to create value – today and for the future.

We are a private bank, offering personalised solutions on a global scale to private and institutional clients. Our sustainable success is based on our talents and on how we partner with our clients and communities to create lasting value.

sections.heading: Role
paragraphs: The Head of Structured Solutions Asia leads the Distribution of Structured Products in Asia. The position involves promoting, developing and implementing structured solutions distribution strategies for the Asian market, leading the product offering and achieve commercial objectives, collaborating with various departments to ensure effective implementation, driving innovation, ensuring compliance, monitoring product and market trends, fostering continuous learning and development, building and maintaining relationships with client facing employees and stakeholders, and identifying/mitigating risks.

jobs.title: Head of Trust Services
jobs.description:
Our Company

EFG International is a global private banking group, offering private banking and asset management services. We serve clients in over 40 locations worldwide. EFG International offers a stimulating and dynamic work environment and strives to be an employer of choice.

EFG Wealth Solutions is part of EFG International. EFG Wealth Solutions (Jersey) Limited ("EFGIJ"), together with its Jersey subsidiaries, are regulated by the Jersey Financial Services Commission and provide Trust Company Business, and Fund Services Business services to our client base.

Our Purpose and Mission

Empowering entrepreneurial minds to create value - today and for the future.

Purpose of Position

To assume the role of Head of Trust Services, Jersey (the “Division”). This is a designated “senior management function” role as defined within the Financial Services Commission (Jersey) Law 1998 and you will discharge the obligations under this regime and in respect of a principal person satisfactorily and in line with defined business activities.

The Head of Trust Services, Jersey is responsible for driving organisation success by setting the strategic direction, having governance oversight, ensuring operational alignment and delivering results that support long term growth and sustainability.

Specifically, you will be directly responsible for leading and developing the Division by setting and implementing strategy for the growth of the business and driving forward the vision, mission, and values of EFG. To exercise accountability to the Board, Global Head of Trust Services, and relevant stakeholders and apply skill and care in exercising your duties, whilst ensuring operation and fiduciary risk exposures are managed to the highest standards and meeting financial performance objectives in line with financial plans.

To drive forward continuous growth in the business, generating meaningful and substantial business, revenue, and profitability growth in line with agreed budget and targets.

As a member of the executive board of EFGIJ, the role holder will drive business performance, foster a culture of excellence and integrity, and contribute to the broader strategic development of EFGIJ.

The position serves as a key liaison with regulators, clients and other stakeholders, ensuring that EFGIJ maintains its reputation as a trusted and leading fiduciary services provider.

Principal Responsibilities

Strategy

- Contribute to the overall strategy, structure and prosperity of your Division. Actively participate in developing, implementing, and communicating the Division's agreed business plan/strategy. Contribute to the management of the local EFG business by acting as a director on the EFG in-house regulated companies, subsidiaries and as a committee member of in-house committees as required.

- Lead, direct and manage the Division to ensure that it meets its regulatory, risk, financial and other stated objectives whilst also promoting and supporting the aims of EFG. Have the ability to strategically plan and proactively direct the Division and workloads to meet the needs of the business. Demonstrate and be recognised as the highest authority, delivering technical excellence in your specialist area and expertise in other relevant business and operational areas.

- Focus on the development, maintenance, and growth of the Division, ensuring the delivery of high-quality fiduciary services, consistency, and operational efficiency in full compliance with regulatory obligations and internal risk frameworks.

- Develop high level strategies for growth, efficiency, and competitive advantage. Demonstrate the ability to simultaneously be entrepreneurial, generating innovative ideas that are strategically sound and drive the company forward, maintaining a focus on the commercial needs, while keeping it under prudent control and acting responsibly towards employees, clients, and intermediaries.

Leadership

- Accountable for the administration, operations, and accounting function within the Division, lead, provide support, encourage innovation, and motivate the management team in their day-today management of the wider administration, operations, and accounting teams. Monitoring of quality and accurate administration and output. Balance operational management and strategic initiatives.

- Focus on leadership development, succession planning, and cultivating a high-performance culture. Build commitment with the administration, operations and accounting teams through fair, consistent, and persuasive behaviour, championing ideas and contributing to the setting of realistic yet challenging objectives through appraisal and other performance measurement initiatives.

- Oversee resourcing, retention, and development in your Division. Develop commitment from your team and other colleagues through recognising good performance, providing timely and constructive feedback and through leading by example. Demonstrate the ability to lead and direct the efforts of others to achieve results.

- Provide support to the Managing Director (Global Head of Trust Services) on any ad-hoc duties as required from time to time.

Relationship Management

- Work collaboratively as part of the senior management team to support, cascade and embed business decisions, always behaving with the utmost integrity and professionalism.

- Develop and maintain effective working relationships with all stakeholders. Play a key role in cross-functional collaboration. Communicate clearly and effectively, using authority and influence appropriately.

- Inspire confidence in new and existing clients by working with key decision makers and influencers to build strong and effective relationships. Build, maintain and deepen strong relationships and credibility with colleagues, clients, and intermediaries by being approachable and proactive in sharing knowledge. Encourage teambuilding, collaboration, and a positive work culture.

Business Development

- Drive forward and support business growth by the development of new business strategic plans and work with colleagues to attract opportunities and onboard new business from wider Group, external introductions, and prospective clients, supported by travelling to clients, and intermediaries as required. Act as one of the primary points of contact for new business enquiries, to include overseeing new business proposals and pitch documentation for prospective clients and/or intermediaries together with the senior management team.

- Drive the achievement of the Division's budget and new business targets through proactive onboarding of new client relationships and the ongoing management and development of existing client relationships.

- Maintain an awareness of the wider Business' and Division's service capabilities and create strategic alliances with third parties. Be a role model and ambassador for EFG both internally and externally, attending conferences, networking events and other relevant opportunities.

- Maintain a knowledge of the external business environment and relevant industry specific trends, both locally and in other jurisdictions in which we have exposure, so that the range of services offered to clients is continually reviewed and to provide sound and practical advice both to colleagues and clients.

Financial

- To drive forward change across the Division, which can be directly linked to increased profitability. Develop cost efficiencies through the introduction of new procedures, systems, working practices, improved financial management and other initiatives.

- Lead commercial reviews of specific areas of the Division and provide reviews of findings and suggested action plans to the CEO EFGIJ.

- Provide input to Divisional budgets and ensure financial results are monitored and continually compared with targets and activities are adjusted to ensure targets are met.

Operational

- Attend, constructively contribute and chair internal committees/meetings as required from time to time and ensuring the timely and accurate reporting of key information where relevant. Act as a director/trustee of client entities as required.

- Act as a point of contact for the administration, operations and accounting teams on technical and other matters within the Division. Demonstrate excellent knowledge of corporate governance requirements, internal compliance, anti-money laundering and other relevant policies and procedures.

- Promote highest standards of client service administration and fiduciary services activities, ensuring that appropriate oversight is maintained thereby allowing fiduciary and operational risk to be effectively managed and controlled.

- Work with the senior management team to identify areas to improve the client experience and implement operational efficiencies, ensuring that the Division is operating efficiently and effectively as possible. Contribute to and maintain adequate policies and procedures for the work conducted by the Division ensuring that these are aligned to EFG expected documentation standards.

- Oversee resourcing, retention, and development in your function/team.

- Act as an “A” Authorised Signatory

- Take responsibility for own personal development, in line with agreed annual performance objectives.

Compliance & Risk

- Contribute to and oversee the implementation of effective risk management frameworks.

- Ensure clients are administered in line with EFG's risk management framework, structure specific and fiduciary risks are mitigated as appropriate. Comply with all local regulatory requirements including, where appropriate, laws, orders, codes of practice and guidance, Group and local policies and procedures relevant to the Business.

- Ensure that all CPD requirements are met year on year for both you and all members of your team, and that training and development is relevant.

Qualifications, skills and experience

- Recognised Professional Qualification - e.g. Accountancy, Chartered Secretary, STEP, Banking, Securities, Compliance or other relevant Table 4 qualification along with relevant experience as defined by the Jersey Financial Services Commission

- 8 years' relevant finance industry experience working within an offshore financial services organisation in a similar role, with a strong technical understanding of all areas of client administration and fiduciary services.

- Focussing on the development, maintenance, and growth of the [Division/Function] business, ensure quality levels of client service, consistency and operational efficiency. Ability to manage complexity and lead large-scale initiatives.

- Comprehensive and detailed understanding of company law, current industry legislation, regulation and working practices and having due regard to corporate governance, compliance, and risk management aspects.

- A good understanding of company financial management.

- Experience of managing and leading people, driving a high-performance culture. Ability to communicate with other areas of the business and key stakeholders in a confident and professional manner.

- Strong leadership and interpersonal skills; approachable and accessible with the ability to inspire, motivate and develop others, and manage underperformance quickly and fairly.

- Proficient in the use of a range of Microsoft products (Outlook, Word, Excel and PowerPoint).

Our Values

- Accountability: Taking ownership for tasks and challenges, as well as seeking continuous improvement

- Hands-on: Being proactive to rapidly deliver high-quality results

- Passionate: Being committed and striving for excellence

- Solution-driven: Focusing on client outcomes and treating clients fairly with a risk-aware mindset

- Partnership-oriented: Promoting collaboration and teamwork. Working together with an entrepreneurial spirit.

descriptionByLocale.en:
Our Company

EFG International is a global private banking group, offering private banking and asset management services. We serve clients in over 40 locations worldwide. EFG International offers a stimulating and dynamic work environment and strives to be an employer of choice.

EFG Wealth Solutions is part of EFG International. EFG Wealth Solutions (Jersey) Limited ("EFGIJ"), together with its Jersey subsidiaries, are regulated by the Jersey Financial Services Commission and provide Trust Company Business, and Fund Services Business services to our client base.

Our Purpose and Mission

Empowering entrepreneurial minds to create value - today and for the future.

Purpose of Position

To assume the role of Head of Trust Services, Jersey (the “Division”). This is a designated “senior management function” role as defined within the Financial Services Commission (Jersey) Law 1998 and you will discharge the obligations under this regime and in respect of a principal person satisfactorily and in line with defined business activities.

The Head of Trust Services, Jersey is responsible for driving organisation success by setting the strategic direction, having governance oversight, ensuring operational alignment and delivering results that support long term growth and sustainability.

Specifically, you will be directly responsible for leading and developing the Division by setting and implementing strategy for the growth of the business and driving forward the vision, mission, and values of EFG. To exercise accountability to the Board, Global Head of Trust Services, and relevant stakeholders and apply skill and care in exercising your duties, whilst ensuring operation and fiduciary risk exposures are managed to the highest standards and meeting financial performance objectives in line with financial plans.

To drive forward continuous growth in the business, generating meaningful and substantial business, revenue, and profitability growth in line with agreed budget and targets.

As a member of the executive board of EFGIJ, the role holder will drive business performance, foster a culture of excellence and integrity, and contribute to the broader strategic development of EFGIJ.

The position serves as a key liaison with regulators, clients and other stakeholders, ensuring that EFGIJ maintains its reputation as a trusted and leading fiduciary services provider.

Principal Responsibilities

Strategy
- Contribute to the overall strategy, structure and prosperity of your Division. Actively participate in developing, implementing, and communicating the Division's agreed business plan/strategy. Contribute to the management of the local EFG business by acting as a director on the EFG in-house regulated companies, subsidiaries and as a committee member of in-house committees as required.
- Lead, direct and manage the Division to ensure that it meets its regulatory, risk, financial and other stated objectives whilst also promoting and supporting the aims of EFG. Have the ability to strategically plan and proactively direct the Division and workloads to meet the needs of the business. Demonstrate and be recognised as the highest authority, delivering technical excellence in your specialist area and expertise in other relevant business and operational areas.
- Focus on the development, maintenance, and growth of the Division, ensuring the delivery of high-quality fiduciary services, consistency, and operational efficiency in full compliance with regulatory obligations and internal risk frameworks.
- Develop high level strategies for growth, efficiency, and competitive advantage. Demonstrate the ability to simultaneously be entrepreneurial, generating innovative ideas that are strategically sound and drive the company forward, maintaining a focus on the commercial needs, while keeping it under prudent control and acting responsibly towards employees, clients, and intermediaries.

Leadership
- Accountable for the administration, operations, and accounting function within the Division, lead, provide support, encourage innovation, and motivate the management team in their day-today management of the wider administration, operations, and accounting teams. Monitoring of quality and accurate administration and output. Balance operational management and strategic initiatives.
- Focus on leadership development, succession planning, and cultivating a high-performance culture. Build commitment with the administration, operations and accounting teams through fair, consistent, and persuasive behaviour, championing ideas and contributing to the setting of realistic yet challenging objectives through appraisal and other performance measurement initiatives.
- Oversee resourcing, retention, and development in your Division. Develop commitment from your team and other colleagues through recognising good performance, providing timely and constructive feedback and through leading by example. Demonstrate the ability to lead and direct the efforts of others to achieve results.
- Provide support to the Managing Director (Global Head of Trust Services) on any ad-hoc duties as required from time to time.

Relationship Management
- Work collaboratively as part of the senior management team to support, cascade and embed business decisions, always behaving with the utmost integrity and professionalism.
- Develop and maintain effective working relationships with all stakeholders. Play a key role in cross-functional collaboration. Communicate clearly and effectively, using authority and influence appropriately.
- Inspire confidence in new and existing clients by working with key decision makers and influencers to build strong and effective relationships. Build, maintain and deepen strong relationships and credibility with colleagues, clients, and intermediaries by being approachable and proactive in sharing knowledge. Encourage teambuilding, collaboration, and a positive work culture.

Business Development
- Drive forward and support business growth by the development of new business strategic plans and work with colleagues to attract opportunities and onboard new business from wider Group, external introductions, and prospective clients, supported by travelling to clients, and intermediaries as required. Act as one of the primary points of contact for new business enquiries, to include overseeing new business proposals and pitch documentation for prospective clients and/or intermediaries together with the senior management team.
- Drive the achievement of the Division's budget and new business targets through proactive onboarding of new client relationships and the ongoing management and development of existing client relationships.
- Maintain an awareness of the wider Business' and Division's service capabilities and create strategic alliances with third parties. Be a role model and ambassador for EFG both internally and externally, attending conferences, networking events and other relevant opportunities.
- Maintain a knowledge of the external business environment and relevant industry specific trends, both locally and in other jurisdictions in which we have exposure, so that the range of services offered to clients is continually reviewed and to provide sound and practical advice both to colleagues and clients.

Financial
- To drive forward change across the Division, which can be directly linked to increased profitability. Develop cost efficiencies through the introduction of new procedures, systems, working practices, improved financial management and other initiatives.
- Lead commercial reviews of specific areas of the Division and provide reviews of findings and suggested action plans to the CEO EFGIJ.
- Provide input to Divisional budgets and ensure financial results are monitored and continually compared with targets and activities are adjusted to ensure targets are met.

Operational
- Attend, constructively contribute and chair internal committees/meetings as required from time to time and ensuring the timely and accurate reporting of key information where relevant. Act as a director/trustee of client entities as required.
- Act as a point of contact for the administration, operations and accounting teams on technical and other matters within the Division. Demonstrate excellent knowledge of corporate governance requirements, internal compliance, anti-money laundering and other relevant policies and procedures.
- Promote highest standards of client service administration and fiduciary services activities, ensuring that appropriate oversight is maintained thereby allowing fiduciary and operational risk to be effectively managed and controlled.
- Work with the senior management team to identify areas to improve the client experience and implement operational efficiencies, ensuring that the Division is operating efficiently and effectively as possible. Contribute to and maintain adequate policies and procedures for the work conducted by the Division ensuring that these are aligned to EFG expected documentation standards.
- Oversee resourcing, retention, and development in your function/team.
- Act as an “A” Authorised Signatory
- Take responsibility for own personal development, in line with agreed annual performance objectives.

Compliance & Risk
- Contribute to and oversee the implementation of effective risk management frameworks.
- Ensure clients are administered in line with EFG's risk management framework, structure specific and fiduciary risks are mitigated as appropriate. Comply with all local regulatory requirements including, where appropriate, laws, orders, codes of practice and guidance, Group and local policies and procedures relevant to the Business.
- Ensure that all CPD requirements are met year on year for both you and all members of your team, and that training and development is relevant.

Qualifications, skills and experience
- Recognised Professional Qualification - e.g. Accountancy, Chartered Secretary, STEP, Banking, Securities, Compliance or other relevant Table 4 qualification along with relevant experience as defined by the Jersey Financial Services Commission

- 8 years' relevant finance industry experience working within an offshore financial services organisation in a similar role, with a strong technical understanding of all areas of client administration and fiduciary services.
- Focussing on the development, maintenance, and growth of the [Division/Function] business, ensure quality levels of client service, consistency and operational efficiency. Ability to manage complexity and lead large-scale initiatives.
- Comprehensive and detailed understanding of company law, current industry legislation, regulation and working practices and having due regard to corporate governance, compliance, and risk management aspects.
- A good understanding of company financial management.
- Experience of managing and leading people, driving a high-performance culture. Ability to communicate with other areas of the business and key stakeholders in a confident and professional manner.
- Strong leadership and interpersonal skills; approachable and accessible with the ability to inspire, motivate and develop others, and manage underperformance quickly and fairly.
- Proficient in the use of a range of Microsoft products (Outlook, Word, Excel and PowerPoint).

Our Values
- Accountability: Taking ownership for tasks and challenges, as well as seeking continuous improvement
- Hands-on: Being proactive to rapidly deliver high-quality results
- Passionate: Being committed and striving for excellence
- Solution-driven: Focusing on client outcomes and treating clients fairly with a risk-aware mindset
- Partnership-oriented: Promoting collaboration and teamwork. Working together with an entrepreneurial spirit.
descriptionByLocale.it:
La nostra società EFG International è un gruppo bancario privato globale, che offre servizi di private banking e gestione patrimoniale. Serviamo clienti in oltre 40 sedi in tutto il mondo. EFG International offre un ambiente di lavoro stimolante e dinamico e si sforza di essere un datore di lavoro di scelta. EFG Wealth Solutions fa parte di EFG International. EFG Wealth Solutions (Jersey) Limited ("EFGIJ"), insieme alle sue controllate di Jersey, sono regolate dalla Commissione dei Servizi Finanziari di Jersey e forniscono servizi Trust Company Business e Fund Services Business alla nostra base clienti. Il nostro scopo e la nostra missione che alimentano le menti imprenditoriali per creare valore - oggi e per il futuro. Scopo di Posizione Per assumere il ruolo di Capo dei Servizi Fiduciari, Jersey (la “Divisione”). Si tratta di un ruolo designato “funzione di gestione aziendale” come definito all’interno della legge sui servizi finanziari della Commissione (Jersey) del 1998 e si assolverà agli obblighi derivanti da questo regime e per una persona principale in modo soddisfacente e in linea con le attività aziendali definite. The Head of Trust Services, Jersey è responsabile della guida del successo dell'organizzazione impostando la direzione strategica, avendo una supervisione della governance, garantendo l'allineamento operativo e offrendo risultati che supportano la crescita a lungo termine e la sostenibilità. In particolare, sarete direttamente responsabili per guidare e sviluppare la Divisione impostando e implementando la strategia per la crescita del business e spingendo avanti la visione, la missione e i valori di EFG. Per esercitare la responsabilità verso il Consiglio, il Global Head of Trust Services e le parti interessate pertinenti e applicare le competenze e le cure nell'esercizio delle proprie funzioni, assicurando al contempo che le esposizioni di rischio operative e fiduciarie siano gestite ai più elevati standard e soddisfano gli obiettivi di performance finanziarie in linea con i piani finanziari. Promuovere la crescita continua del business, generando una crescita significativa e sostanziale del business, del fatturato e della redditività in linea con il bilancio concordato e obiettivi. Come membro del consiglio esecutivo di EFGIJ, il titolare del ruolo guiderà le prestazioni aziendali, incoraggerà una cultura di eccellenza e integrità, e contribuirà allo sviluppo strategico più ampio di EFGIJ. La posizione funge da collegamento chiave con i regolatori, i clienti e altri stakeholder, assicurando che EFGIJ mantiene la sua reputazione come fornitore di servizi fiduciari affidabile e leader. Strategia Principali Responsabilità
- Contribuisci alla strategia generale, alla struttura e alla prosperità della tua Divisione. Partecipa attivamente allo sviluppo, all'implementazione e alla comunicazione del business plan/strategy concordato della Divisione. Contribuire alla gestione del business locale EFG agendo come direttore delle società regolamentate EFG in-house, controllate e come membro del comitato dei comitati interni come richiesto.
- Guidare, dirigere e gestire la Divisione per garantire che soddisfi i suoi obiettivi normativi, di rischio, finanziari e di altro tipo, promuovendo e sostenendo anche gli obiettivi di EFG. Avere la capacità di pianificare strategicamente e dirigere proattivamente la Divisione e carichi di lavoro per soddisfare le esigenze del business. Dimostrare e essere riconosciuto come la più alta autorità, offrendo l'eccellenza tecnica nella vostra area specializzata e competenze in altre aree aziendali e operative rilevanti.
- Focus sullo sviluppo, la manutenzione e la crescita della Divisione, garantendo la fornitura di servizi fiduciari di alta qualità, la coerenza e l'efficienza operativa nel pieno rispetto degli obblighi normativi e dei quadri di rischio interni.
- Sviluppare strategie di alto livello per la crescita, l'efficienza e il vantaggio competitivo. Dimostrare la capacità di essere contemporaneamente imprenditoriali, generando idee innovative che sono strategicamente solide e spingono l'azienda avanti, mantenendo un focus sulle esigenze commerciali, mantenendolo sotto controllo prudente e agendo responsabilmente verso dipendenti, clienti e intermediari. Leadership
- Contabile per l'amministrazione, le operazioni e la funzione contabile all'interno della Divisione, guidare, fornire supporto, incoraggiare l'innovazione e motivare il team di gestione nella gestione quotidiana delle più ampie squadre di amministrazione, gestione e contabilità. Monitoraggio della qualità e gestione accurata e dell'output. Equilibrio gestione operativa e iniziative strategiche.
- Concentrati sullo sviluppo della leadership, sulla pianificazione della successione e sulla coltivazione di una cultura ad alte prestazioni. Costruire l'impegno con i team di amministrazione, gestione e contabilità attraverso un comportamento equo, coerente e persuasivo, sostenendo idee e contribuendo alla definizione di obiettivi realistici ma impegnativi attraverso iniziative di valutazione e altre misure di performance.
- Superare la risonanza, la ritenzione e lo sviluppo nella vostra Divisione. Sviluppare l'impegno del tuo team e di altri colleghi attraverso il riconoscimento di buone prestazioni, fornendo feedback tempestivi e costruttivi e attraverso l'esempio di leader. Dimostrare la capacità di condurre e dirigere gli sforzi degli altri per raggiungere i risultati.
- Fornire supporto all'amministratore delegato (Global Head of Trust Services) su eventuali obblighi ad hoc richiesti di volta in volta. Relationship Management
- Lavorare collaborativamente come parte del team di senior management per sostenere, cascata e prendere decisioni aziendali, comportandosi sempre con la massima integrità e professionalità.
- Sviluppare e mantenere relazioni di lavoro efficaci con tutti gli stakeholder. Riproduci un ruolo chiave nella collaborazione interfunzionale. Comunicare con chiarezza ed efficacia, utilizzando autorità e influenza in modo appropriato.
- Ispirare la fiducia nei clienti nuovi ed esistenti lavorando con i decisori chiave e gli influencer per costruire relazioni forti ed efficaci. Costruire, mantenere e approfondire relazioni forti e credibilità con colleghi, clienti e intermediari, avvicinandosi e proattivando la condivisione della conoscenza. Incoraggia il team building, la collaborazione e una cultura del lavoro positiva. Sviluppo aziendale
- Promuovere e sostenere la crescita aziendale attraverso lo sviluppo di nuovi piani strategici di business e lavorare con i colleghi per attirare opportunità e a bordo di nuovi business da più ampio Gruppo, introduzioni esterne e potenziali clienti, supportati da viaggi ai clienti e agli intermediari come richiesto. Agire come uno dei punti di contatto principali per le nuove richieste di business, per includere la supervisione di nuove proposte di business e la documentazione di pitch per i potenziali clienti e/o intermediari insieme al team di gestione senior. - guidare il raggiungimento del bilancio della Divisione e nuovi obiettivi di business attraverso la gestione proattiva di nuove relazioni con i clienti e la gestione e lo sviluppo in corso delle relazioni con i clienti esistenti.
- Mantenere la consapevolezza delle più ampie capacità di servizio di Business e Divisione e creare alleanze strategiche con terzi. Essere un modello di ruolo e ambasciatore per EFG sia internamente che esternamente, partecipare a conferenze, eventi di networking e altre opportunità rilevanti.
- Mantenere una conoscenza dell'ambiente di business esterno e delle tendenze specifiche del settore rilevanti, sia localmente che in altre giurisdizioni in cui abbiamo esposizione, in modo che la gamma di servizi offerti ai clienti sia costantemente riesaminata e fornire consulenza sana e pratica sia a colleghi e clienti. Finanziario
- Per guidare il cambiamento in avanti in tutta la Divisione, che può essere direttamente collegato ad una maggiore redditività. Sviluppare l'efficienza dei costi attraverso l'introduzione di nuove procedure, sistemi, pratiche di lavoro, una migliore gestione finanziaria e altre iniziative.
- Piombo recensioni commerciali di aree specifiche della Divisione e fornire recensioni di risultati e piani d'azione suggeriti al CEO EFGIJ.
- Fornire input ai bilanci di divisione e garantire che i risultati finanziari vengano monitorati e continuamente confrontati con gli obiettivi e le attività sono regolate per garantire il raggiungimento degli obiettivi. Operativo
- Partecipare, contribuire in modo costruttivo e presiedere comitati / incontri interni, come richiesto di volta in volta e garantire la tempestiva e accurata segnalazione di informazioni chiave laddove pertinenti. Agire come direttore/fidenza delle entità client come richiesto.
- Agire come punto di contatto per l'amministrazione, le operazioni e i team contabili su questioni tecniche e di altro tipo all'interno della Divisione. Dimostrare un'ottima conoscenza dei requisiti di corporate governance, la conformità interna, il riciclaggio di denaro e altre politiche e procedure pertinenti.
- Promuovere i più elevati standard delle attività di amministrazione del servizio clienti e dei servizi fiduciari, assicurando che sia mantenuta una corretta supervisione, consentendo così la gestione e il controllo del rischio fiduciario e operativo.
- Lavorare con il team di gestione senior per identificare le aree per migliorare l'esperienza del cliente e implementare le efficienze operative, assicurando che la Divisione funzioni nel modo più efficiente ed efficace possibile. Contribuire e mantenere adeguate politiche e procedure per il lavoro svolto dalla Divisione assicurando che queste siano allineate agli standard di documentazione previsti dall'EFG.
- Superare la risonanza, la ritenzione e lo sviluppo nella vostra funzione / team.
- Agire come firmatario autorizzato "A"
- Assumersi la responsabilità per il proprio sviluppo personale, in linea con gli obiettivi di prestazione annuali concordati. Compliance & Risk
- Contribuire e supervisionare l'implementazione di efficaci framework di gestione dei rischi.
- Assicurare che i clienti siano amministrati in linea con il quadro di gestione del rischio di EFG, i rischi specifici e fiduciari della struttura vengono mitigati nel modo appropriato. Complimenti a tutti i requisiti normativi locali, tra cui, se del caso, leggi, ordini, codici di pratica e guida, politiche e procedure di Gruppo e locali pertinenti al Business.
- Assicurarsi che tutti i requisiti della CPD siano soddisfatti anno per anno sia per voi che per tutti i membri del vostro team, e che la formazione e lo sviluppo sia rilevante. Qualifiche, competenze ed esperienza
- Riconosciuto Qualifica professionale - ad esempio Contabilità, Segreteria Chartered, STEP, Banking, Securities, Compliance o altre qualificazioni Tabella 4 rilevanti insieme a un'esperienza rilevante come definita dalla Commissione dei Servizi Finanziari di Jersey - esperienza di settore finanziario di 8 anni che lavora all'interno di un'organizzazione di servizi finanziari offshore in un ruolo simile, con una forte comprensione tecnica di tutti i settori dell'amministrazione clienti e dei servizi fiduciari.
- Concentrandosi sullo sviluppo, la manutenzione e la crescita del business [Division/Function], assicurano livelli di qualità di servizio client, consistenza ed efficienza operativa. Capacità di gestire la complessità e condurre iniziative su larga scala.
- Comprensione completa e dettagliata del diritto societario, dell'attuale legislazione del settore, della regolamentazione e delle pratiche di lavoro e tenuto conto degli aspetti di corporate governance, compliance e risk management.
- Una buona comprensione della gestione finanziaria aziendale.
- Esperienza di gestione e leadership, guidando una cultura ad alte prestazioni. Capacità di comunicare con altre aree del business e le parti interessate chiave in modo sicuro e professionale.
- Forte leadership e competenze interpersonali; accessibile e accessibile con la capacità di ispirare, motivare e sviluppare gli altri, e gestire le prestazioni in modo rapido e corretto.
- Proficiente nell'uso di una gamma di prodotti Microsoft (Outlook, Word, Excel e PowerPoint). I nostri valori
- Responsabilità: Prendere la proprietà per i compiti e le sfide, così come la ricerca di un miglioramento continuo
- Hands-on: Essere proattivi a fornire rapidamente risultati di alta qualità
- Passionate: Essere impegnati e impegnati per l'eccellenza
- Solution-driven: Focusing sui risultati del cliente e trattare i clienti abbastanza con una mentalità di rischio-consapevole
- Partnership-oriented: Promuovere la collaborazione e il lavoro di squadra. Lavorare insieme a uno spirito imprenditoriale.
descriptionByLocale.de:
Unser Unternehmen EFG International ist eine globale Privatbankgruppe, die private Bank- und Asset-Management-Dienste anbietet. Wir servieren Kunden an über 40 Standorten weltweit. EFG International bietet ein anregendes und dynamisches Arbeitsumfeld und strebt danach, Arbeitgeber der Wahl zu sein. EFG Wealth Solutions ist Teil der EFG International. EFG Wealth Solutions (Jersey) Limited ("EFGIJ"), zusammen mit seinen Jersey-Tochtergesellschaften, werden von der Jersey Financial Services Commission reguliert und bieten Trust Company Business und Fund Services Business Services an unsere Kundenbasis. Unsere Aufgabe und Mission, unternehmerische Köpfe zu stärken, um Wert zu schaffen - heute und für die Zukunft. Zweck der Position Um die Rolle des Head of Trust Services, Jersey (die “Division”) zu übernehmen. Dies ist eine benannte „senior management function“-Rolle, die im Rahmen des Financial Services Commission (Jersey) Gesetz 1998 definiert ist und Sie werden die Verpflichtungen aus diesem System und in Bezug auf eine Hauptperson zufrieden stellend und im Einklang mit definierten Geschäftstätigkeiten entlasten. Der Head of Trust Services, Jersey, ist für den Erfolg der Organisation verantwortlich, indem er die strategische Ausrichtung festlegt, die Governance-Übersicht besitzt, die operative Ausrichtung gewährleistet und Ergebnisse liefert, die langfristiges Wachstum und Nachhaltigkeit unterstützen. Insbesondere werden Sie direkt für die Führung und Entwicklung der Division verantwortlich sein, indem Sie Strategie für das Wachstum des Unternehmens festlegen und implementieren und die Vision, Mission und Werte der EFG vorantreiben. Um die Rechenschaftspflicht gegenüber dem Board, dem Global Head of Trust Services und den relevanten Stakeholdern auszuüben und Fähigkeiten und Sorgfalt bei der Ausübung Ihrer Aufgaben anzuwenden, werden gleichzeitig sichergestellt, dass Operationen und fiduziäre Risikopositionen den höchsten Standards und der Erfüllung der finanziellen Leistungsziele im Einklang mit den Finanzplänen verwaltet werden. Um ein kontinuierliches Wachstum im Unternehmen voranzutreiben und ein aussagekräftiges und substantielles Geschäfts-, Umsatz- und Ertragswachstum im Einklang mit vereinbartem Budget und Zielen zu erzielen. Als Mitglied des Vorstands der EFGIJ wird der Rolleninhaber die Geschäftsentwicklung vorantreiben, eine Kultur der Exzellenz und Integrität fördern und zur breiteren strategischen Entwicklung des EFGIJ beitragen. Die Position dient als zentrale Verbindung zu Regulierungsbehörden, Kunden und anderen Interessenvertretern, um sicherzustellen, dass EFGIJ seinen Ruf als vertrauenswürdiger und führender Anbieter von Finanzdienstleistungen behält. Hauptverantwortliche Strategie
- Beitrag zur Gesamtstrategie, Struktur und Wohlstand Ihrer Division. aktiv an der Entwicklung, Umsetzung und Kommunikation des vereinbarten Geschäftsplans/Strategie der Division beteiligt. Beitrag zur Verwaltung des lokalen EFG-Geschäfts, indem er als Direktor der EFG-intern regulierten Unternehmen, Tochtergesellschaften und als Ausschussmitglied der hausinternen Ausschüsse tätig ist.
- Führen, leiten und verwalten Sie die Division, um sicherzustellen, dass sie ihre regulatorischen, Risiko-, Finanz- und anderen festgelegten Ziele erfüllt und gleichzeitig die Ziele der EFG fördert und unterstützt. Haben Sie die Fähigkeit, die Division und Workloads strategisch zu planen und proaktiv zu leiten, um die Bedürfnisse des Unternehmens zu erfüllen. Demonstrieren und als höchste Behörde anerkannt werden und technische Exzellenz in Ihrem Fachgebiet und Ihrer Expertise in anderen relevanten Geschäfts- und Betriebsbereichen bieten.
- Fokussieren Sie auf die Entwicklung, Wartung und Wachstum der Division, um die Lieferung hochwertiger treuhänderischer Dienstleistungen, Konsistenz und operativer Effizienz in voller Übereinstimmung mit regulatorischen Verpflichtungen und internen Risikorahmen zu gewährleisten.
- Entwicklung hochrangiger Strategien für Wachstum, Effizienz und Wettbewerbsvorteile. Demonstrieren Sie die Fähigkeit, gleichzeitig unternehmerisch zu sein, innovative Ideen zu generieren, die strategisch gesund sind und das Unternehmen vorantreiben, einen Fokus auf die kommerziellen Bedürfnisse zu halten, während es unter vorsichtiger Kontrolle und verantwortungsvoll gegenüber Mitarbeitern, Kunden und Vermittlern zu handeln. Leadership
- Verantwortlich für die Verwaltung, den Betrieb und die Rechnungslegung innerhalb der Division, führen, unterstützen, fördern Innovation und motivieren das Management-Team in ihrer täglichen Verwaltung der breiteren Verwaltungs-, Betriebs- und Rechnungsführungsteams. Qualitätskontrolle und genaue Verwaltung und Ausgabe. Balance operatives Management und strategische Initiativen.
- Fokus auf Führungsentwicklung, Nachfolgeplanung und Kultivierung einer leistungsfähigen Kultur. Aufbauen Sie durch ein faires, konsequentes und überzeugendes Verhalten mit den Verwaltungs-, Betriebs- und Rechnungsführungsteams, die Ideen und den Beitrag zur Einstellung realistischer, aber anspruchsvoller Ziele durch Bewertungs- und andere Leistungsmessinitiativen.
- Überwachen Sie die Beschaffung, Retention und Entwicklung in Ihrer Abteilung. Entwickeln Sie Engagement von Ihrem Team und anderen Kollegen, indem Sie eine gute Leistung erkennen, zeitnah und konstruktives Feedback und durch ein Beispiel führen. Demonstrieren Sie die Fähigkeit, die Bemühungen anderer zu führen und zu leiten, um Ergebnisse zu erzielen.
- UnterstÃ1⁄4tzt den Geschäftsführer (Global Head of Trust Services) Ã1⁄4ber alle Ad-hoc-Funktionen, die von Zeit zu Zeit erforderlich sind. Relationship Management
- Arbeiten Sie gemeinsam als Teil des Senior-Management-Teams, um Geschäftsentscheidungen zu unterstützen, Kaskade und einzubetten, immer mit größter Integrität und Professionalität.
- Entwicklung und Aufrechterhaltung effektiver Arbeitsbeziehungen mit allen Beteiligten. Spielen Sie eine Schlüsselrolle in der funktionsübergreifenden Zusammenarbeit. Kommunikation klar und effektiv, mit Autorität und Einfluss entsprechend.
- Inspirieren Sie das Vertrauen in neue und bestehende Kunden, indem Sie mit den Entscheidern und Influencern zusammenarbeiten, um starke und effektive Beziehungen aufzubauen. Aufbauen, pflegen und vertiefen starke Beziehungen und Glaubwürdigkeit mit Kollegen, Kunden und Mittlern, indem sie sich nähern und proaktiv im Wissensaustausch sind. Ermutigen Sie Teambuilding, Zusammenarbeit und eine positive Arbeitskultur. Business Development
- Fahren Sie voran und unterstützen Sie das Geschäftswachstum durch die Entwicklung neuer Business-Strategiepläne und arbeiten Sie mit Kollegen zusammen, um Chancen und an Bord Neues von breiter Gruppe, externen Einführungen und potenziellen Kunden zu gewinnen, unterstützt durch Reisen zu Kunden und Intermediäre nach Bedarf. Aktivieren Sie als einer der wichtigsten Ansprechpartner für neue Geschäftsanfragen, um neue Geschäftsvorschläge und Pitch-Dokumentation für potenzielle Kunden und/oder Vermittler zusammen mit dem Senior Management-Team zu überwachen.
- Führen Sie die Erreichung des Budgets und der neuen Geschäftsziele der Division durch ein proaktives An Bord neuer Kundenbeziehungen und das laufende Management und die Entwicklung bestehender Kundenbeziehungen.
- Bewahren Sie das Bewusstsein für die Servicefähigkeiten von Business und Division und schaffen Sie strategische Allianzen mit Dritten. Seien Sie ein Vorbild und Botschafter für EFG sowohl intern als auch extern, an Konferenzen, Netzwerkveranstaltungen und anderen relevanten Möglichkeiten.
- Bewahren Sie die Kenntnisse des externen Geschäftsumfelds und der relevanten branchenspezifischen Trends, sowohl lokal als auch in anderen Zuständigkeiten, in denen wir uns befinden, auf, so dass das Angebot an Dienstleistungen für Kunden kontinuierlich überprüft und fundierte und praktische Beratung sowohl für Kollegen als auch für Kunden bietet. Finanzen
- Um den Wandel in der gesamten Division voranzutreiben, was direkt mit einer erhöhten Rentabilität verbunden werden kann. Entwicklung von Kosteneffizienzen durch Einführung neuer Verfahren, Systeme, Arbeitspraktiken, verbessertes Finanzmanagement und andere Initiativen.
- Führen Sie kommerzielle Bewertungen von spezifischen Bereichen der Division und liefern Bewertungen von Ergebnissen und vorgeschlagenen Aktionsplänen für den CEO EFGIJ.
- Bereitstellung von Beiträgen zu den Divisionsbudgets und sicherzustellen, dass die Finanzergebnisse überwacht und kontinuierlich mit Zielen und Aktivitäten verglichen werden, um sicherzustellen, dass Ziele erreicht werden. Operationelles
- Beabsichtigen, konstruktiver Beitrag und Vorsitz interner Ausschüsse/Besprechungen, die von Zeit zu Zeit erforderlich sind und die rechtzeitige und genaue Berichterstattung über Schlüsselinformationen, soweit relevant, gewährleisten. Aktivieren Sie als Director/Truste von Client-Entities nach Bedarf.
- Aktivieren Sie als Ansprechpartner für die Verwaltungs-, Betriebs- und Rechnungsführungsteams in technischen und anderen Angelegenheiten der Division. Demonstrieren Sie hervorragende Kenntnisse über Corporate Governance-Anforderungen, interne Compliance, Anti-Geldwäsche und andere relevante Politiken und Verfahren.
- Förderung der höchsten Standards der Kundendienstverwaltung und der fiduziären Dienstleistungen, um sicherzustellen, dass eine angemessene Aufsicht aufrecht erhalten wird, so dass das fiduziäre und operative Risiko effektiv verwaltet und kontrolliert werden kann.
- Arbeiten Sie mit dem Senior Management-Team zusammen, um Bereiche zu identifizieren, um die Kundenerfahrung zu verbessern und operative Effizienzen umzusetzen, um sicherzustellen, dass die Division effizient und effektiv wie möglich arbeitet. Um angemessene Maßnahmen und Verfahren für die von der Abteilung durchgeführte Arbeit zu gewährleisten und zu erhalten, dass diese auf die von der EFG erwarteten Dokumentationsstandards ausgerichtet sind.
- Überwachen Sie die Beschaffung, Retention und Entwicklung in Ihrer Funktion/Team.
- Gesetz als "A" autorisiertes Unterzeichner
