Skills and experience
- Bachelor’s or Master’s Degree in Economics or Business Administration
- At least 2 years of experience in the banking, insurance, or financial services sector in client/customer services
- Good knowledge of Financial Products and ideally Private Banking
- Advanced Excel skills

Our Values

Accountability: Taking ownership for tasks and challenges, as well as seeking continuous improvement

Hands-on: Being proactive to rapidly deliver high-quality results

Passionate: Being committed and striving for excellence

Solution-driven: Focusing on client outcomes and treating clients fairly with a risk-aware mindset

Partnership-oriented: Promoting collaboration and teamwork. Working together with an entrepreneurial spirit.
descriptionByLocale.it:
La nostra società EFG International è un gruppo bancario privato globale, che offre servizi di private banking e gestione patrimoniale. Serviamo clienti in oltre 40 sedi in tutto il mondo. EFG International offre un ambiente di lavoro stimolante e dinamico e si sforza di essere un datore di lavoro di scelta.

## Il nostro scopo e la nostra missione alimentano le menti imprenditoriali per creare valore – oggi e per il futuro.

## Job Description Nel quadro della First Line of Defense (FLoD), Business Risk Management (BRM) assicura l'efficacia del sistema di controllo interno nell'area PB. Sotto la supervisione del PB COO, BRM organizza ed esegue un certo numero di compiti intorno alle attività di supporto e monitoraggio giornaliero del dipartimento. BRM si concentra sui controlli ex post, supporta il Global BRM nel coordinamento delle attività di gestione dei rischi FLoD, migliora la qualità dell'attività FLoD garantendo che regole e processi siano rispettati in modo efficace, supporta i CRO locali con l'esecuzione dei controlli e agisce come interfaccia tra le funzioni Business Head e 2nd e 3rd LoD. 1. Controlling Eseguire tutti i controlli ex post con attenzione ai processi interni e agli argomenti normativi, in linea con la stampa di controllo PB. Seguire/risoluzione degli avvisi operativi. Eseguire recensioni aggiuntive / ad-hoc come necessario. 2. Relazione sui controlli giornalieri/mesi/quarantali e sul follow-up dei problemi rilevati. Relazione sull'ambiente di controllo interno e rischi o problemi potenzialmente derivanti. Supporta la produzione di CRO Risk Scorecards Eseguire report aggiuntivi / ad-hoc come necessario. 3. Sostegno a migliorare l'efficacia e l'efficienza delle attività di controllo e gestione dei rischi PB. Sostenere le valutazioni dei rischi PB in coordinamento con la seconda linea di difesa. 4. Varie participate in vari progetti di front office, operativi e di risk management come richiesto.

## Abilità ed esperienza
- Laurea triennale o Master in Economia o Business Administration
- Almeno 2 anni di esperienza nel settore bancario, assicurativo o dei servizi finanziari nei servizi client/clienti
- Buona conoscenza dei prodotti finanziari e idealmente Private Banking
- Advanced Excel skills

## Our Values Accountability: Assunzione della proprietà per i compiti e le sfide, così come la ricerca di un miglioramento continuo Hands-on: Essere proattivi a fornire rapidamente risultati di alta qualità Passionate: Essere impegnati e impegnati per l'eccellenza Guidati dalla soluzione: Focusing sui risultati del cliente e trattare i clienti abbastanza con una mentalità di rischio-consapevole Partnership-oriented: Promuovere la collaborazione e il lavoro di squadra. Lavorare insieme a uno spirito imprenditoriale.
descriptionByLocale.de:
Unser Unternehmen EFG International ist eine globale Privatbankgruppe, die private Bank- und Asset-Management-Dienste anbietet. Wir servieren Kunden an über 40 Standorten weltweit. EFG International bietet ein anregendes und dynamisches Arbeitsumfeld und strebt danach, Arbeitgeber der Wahl zu sein. Unsere Aufgabe und Mission, unternehmerische Köpfe zu stärken, um Wert zu schaffen – heute und für die Zukunft. In den Warenkorb Im Rahmen der Ersten Verteidigungslinie (FLoD) sorgt das Business Risk Management (BRM) für die Wirksamkeit des internen Kontrollsystems im PB-Bereich. Unter Aufsicht der PB COO organisiert und führt BRM eine Reihe von Aufgaben rund um die tägliche Unterstützung und Überwachung der Abteilung. BRM konzentriert sich auf Ex-post-Kontrollen, unterstützt den Global BRM bei der Koordinierung der FLoD-Risikomanagementaktivitäten, verbessert die Qualität der FLoD-Aktivitäten, indem gewährleistet wird, dass Regeln und Prozesse effektiv eingehalten werden, unterstützt lokale CROs mit der Ausführung ihrer Kontrollen und fungiert als Schnittstelle zwischen dem Business Head und 2. und 3. LoD-Funktionen. 1. Controlling Execute alle Ex-post-Kontrollen mit Fokus auf interne Prozesse und regulatorische Themen, im Einklang mit PB-Kontroll-Blueprint. Nachfolge/Auflösung von Betriebsalarmen. Führen Sie zusätzliche / ad-hoc Bewertungen nach Bedarf durch. 2. Bericht über die tägliche/monatliche/vierteljährliche Kontrolle und Nachverfolgung der festgestellten Probleme. Bericht über die interne Kontrollumgebung und potenziell auftretende Risiken oder Probleme. Unterstützung der Produktion von CRO Risk Scorecards Führen Sie zusätzliche / ad-hoc Berichterstattung nach Bedarf. 3. Überprüfung Unterstützung bei der Verbesserung der Wirksamkeit und Effizienz der PB-Kontroll- und Risikomanagementaktivitäten. Unterstützung von PB-Risikobewertungen in Abstimmung mit der Zweiten Verteidigungslinie. 4. Verschiedenes Beteiligen Sie sich an verschiedenen Vor-, Betriebs- und Risikomanagement-Projekten wie gewünscht. Fähigkeiten und Erfahrung
- Bachelor- oder Masterstudiengang Wirtschafts- oder Betriebswirtschaft
- Mindestens 2 Jahre Erfahrung im Bereich Bank-, Versicherungs- oder Finanzdienstleistungen in Kunden- und Kundendiensten
- Gute Kenntnisse der Finanzprodukte und ideal Private Banking
- Advanced Excel Fähigkeiten Unsere Werte Rechenschaftspflicht: Unternehmung für Aufgaben und Herausforderungen sowie Suche nach kontinuierlicher Verbesserung Hands-on: Seien Sie proaktiv, um schnell qualitativ hochwertige Ergebnisse liefern Leidenschaft: Engagement und Streben nach Exzellenz Lösungsgetrieben: Fokussieren Sie auf Kundenergebnisse und behandeln Sie Kunden fair mit einem risikobewussten Mindset Partnerschaftsorientierte: Förderung der Zusammenarbeit und Teamarbeit. Zusammenarbeit mit einem Unternehmergeist.
descriptionByLocale.fr:
Notre société EFG International est un groupe bancaire privé mondial offrant des services de banque privée et de gestion d'actifs. Nous servons des clients dans plus de 40 endroits dans le monde. EFG International offre un environnement de travail stimulant et dynamique et s'efforce d'être un employeur de choix.

## Notre but et notre mission Donner aux esprits entrepreneurs les moyens de créer de la valeur – aujourd'hui et pour l'avenir. Description du travail Dans le cadre de la première ligne de défense (FLoD), la gestion des risques commerciaux (BRM) assure l'efficacité du système de contrôle interne dans le domaine de la PB. Sous la supervision du COO du PB, BRM organise et exécute un certain nombre de tâches autour des activités quotidiennes de soutien et de suivi du département. Elle/Il coordonne l'évaluation des risques de PB en étroite adéquation avec le Private Banking Support afin d'obtenir un FloD robuste. BRM met l'accent sur les contrôles ex post, soutient le Global BRM dans la coordination des activités de gestion des risques de FLoD, améliore la qualité de l'activité de FLoD en s'assurant que les règles et les processus sont respectés efficacement, soutient les ORC locaux avec l'exécution de leurs contrôles et agit comme interface entre le Business Head et les fonctions de 2e et 3e LoD. 1. Contrôle Exécuter tous les contrôles ex post en mettant l'accent sur les processus internes et les sujets réglementaires, conformément au plan de contrôle PB. Suivi/résolution des alertes opérationnelles. Effectuer des examens supplémentaires/ad hoc au besoin. 2. Rapport sur les contrôles quotidiens, mensuels et trimestriels et le suivi des problèmes détectés. Rapport sur l'environnement de contrôle interne et les risques ou problèmes potentiels. Soutenir la production de cartes de pointage des risques des ORC Effectuer des rapports supplémentaires/ad hoc au besoin. 3. Examen de l'appui pour améliorer l'efficacité et l'efficience des activités de contrôle et de gestion des risques du BP. Appuyer les évaluations des risques du BP en coordination avec la deuxième ligne de défense. 4. Divers Participer à divers projets de gestion des risques, opérationnels et de front office, selon les besoins.

## Compétences et expérience
- Baccalauréat ou maîtrise en économie ou en administration des affaires
- Au moins 2 ans d'expérience dans le secteur des services bancaires, d'assurance ou financiers dans les services à la clientèle
- Bonne connaissance des produits financiers et idéalement de la banque privée
- Compétences Excel avancées

## Nos valeurs Responsabilité : S'approprier les tâches et les défis, et chercher une amélioration continue Hands-on: Être proactif pour obtenir rapidement des résultats de haute qualité Passionné: S'engager et s'efforcer d'atteindre l'excellence Inspiré par la solution: Se concentrer sur les résultats des clients et traiter les clients équitablement avec un état d'esprit conscient des risques. Travailler ensemble avec un esprit d'entreprise.

summary:
Our Company

EFG International is a global private banking group, offering private banking and asset management services. We serve clients in over 40 locations worldwide. EFG International offers a stimulating and dynamic work environment and strives to be an employer of choice.

EFG is committed to providing an equitable and inclusive working environment that is founded on the principle of mutual respect. Joining our team means experiencing a supportive environment, where your contributions are valued and recognised. We strongly believe that the diversity of our teams gives us a competitive advantage by fostering better decision-making and greater innovation.

Our Purpose and Mission

Empowering entrepreneurial minds to create value – today and for the future.

We are a private bank, offering personalised solutions on a global scale to private and institutional clients. Our sustainable success is based on our talents and on how we partner with our clients and communities to create lasting value.

Job Description

In the framework of the First Line of Defense (FLoD), Business Risk Management (BRM) ensures the effectiveness of the Internal Control System in the PB area. Under the supervision of the PB COO, BRM organizes and performs a certain number of tasks around the daily support & monitoring activities of the department. She/He coordinates the PB risk assessment in close alignment with Private Banking Support to achieve a robust FloD.

BRM focuses on ex-post controls, supports the Global BRM in the coordination of FLoD risk management activities, improves the quality of FLoD activity by ensuring that rules and processes are adhered to effectively, supports local CROs with the execution of their controls and acts as interface between the Business Head and 2nd and 3rd LoD functions.

1. Controlling

Execute all ex-post controls with focus on internal processes and regulatory topics, in line with PB control blueprint. Follow-up/resolution of operational alerts.

Perform additional / ad-hoc reviews as needed.

2. Reporting

Report on daily/monthly/quarterly controls and follow-up of detected issues.

Report on the internal control environment and potentially arising risks or issues.

Support production of CRO Risk Scorecards Perform additional / ad-hoc reporting as needed.

3. Reviewing

Support in enhancing effectiveness and efficiency of PB control and risk management activities.

Support PB risk assessments in coordination with the Second Line of Defence.

4. Miscellaneous

Participate in various front office, operational and risk management projects as requested.

Skills and experience

  • Bachelor’s or Master’s Degree in Economics or Business Administration
  • At least 2 years of experience in the banking, insurance, or financial services sector in client/customer services
  • Good knowledge of Financial Products and ideally Private Banking
  • Advanced Excel skills

Our Values

Accountability: Taking ownership for tasks and challenges, as well as seeking continuous improvement

Hands-on: Being proactive to rapidly deliver high-quality results

Passionate: Being committed and striving for excellence

Solution-driven: Focusing on client outcomes and treating clients fairly with a risk-aware mindset

Partnership-oriented: Promoting collaboration and teamwork. Working together with an entrepreneurial spirit.

jobs.title: Business Risk Manager - Authorized Officer/Associate Director
jobs.description:
## Main Duties

- Accountable for the development of a comprehensive risk and control assessment for all aspects of risk within the FLoD, including the effective oversight of the testing programme, and review this with the business on a regular basis.

-
Responsible for ensuring that a comprehensive risk based control monitoring programme exists across the function, evidencing the adequacy and effectiveness of key controls

-
Perform regular business controls and monitoring on various areas, i.e. Investment Suitability, Cross Border, Anti-Fraud, etc., and produce management reporting on the findings.

-
Conduct thematic reviews, investigations and gap analysis on different aspects within the FLoD.

-
Participate in ad-hoc projects and work closely with stakeholders to implement changes & enhancements.

-
Responsible for the development of a strong risk awareness and client fairness culture across all aspects of the business.

-
Collaborate with internal stakeholders (i.e. Second Line of Defense (SLoD), Global Function) to identify, assess, and mitigate risks across business operations, and to ensure appropriate controls are in place.

-
Accountable for proactive escalation, reporting, provision of advice and challenging existing processes in relation to the business activities.

- Undertakes any other duties within the Business Risk Management or as directed by the Head of Business Risk Management.

- Manages FLOD Risk Scorecard and ensures proper tracking, execution and integrity of the score card.

- Provides counsel to front office staff on investment advisory activities and to ensure compliance with in-house policies & procedures and local regulatory requirements.

- Builds strong relationships and credibility with key internal stakeholders

## Requirements

- Bachelor degree or above in Business Administration, Finance, Economics, or related fields

- 6 years or above of experience in financial or banking industries

- Prior experience with general risk management, including control framework is preferred

- Understanding of products and Wealth regulatory banking environment

- Experienced in liaising with internal business partners

- Demonstrated ability to work with minimal supervision, multi-tasking and to prioritize tasks

jobs.title: Assistant Relationship Manager
jobs.description:
## Main Duties & Responsibilities

- Introduces the Bank and its banking services to prospective clients.

- Provides support to Relationship Manager to achieve their business goals such as NNA / Revenue targets.

- Markets and provides comprehensive private banking services to meet varied wealth management needs.

- Performs KYC / AML due diligence on clients in compliance with the Bank’s general directive and ongoing periodic review from time to time.

- Go through the Investment Risk Profile Questionnaire with clients at time of account opening as well as ongoing periodic review to ensure and update clients’ investment product suitability, risk tolerance and investment experience from time to time.

- Liaises and coordinates with the Bank’s investment units, e.g. Treasury Department and Global Product Unit for passing on product information and markets commentary to clients from time to time.

- Closely monitor clients’ portfolio and account activities to ensure the compliance with internal guidelines as well as external regulations from time to time. Monitor clients’ position to ensure collateral sufficiency at all times and prompt regularization of excesses, if any.

- Prepares and maintains proper records including but not limited to account documentation, CIP, IRPQ, credit application and relevant documents, trade transactions and call reports, etc.

- Accept, confirm and execute clients’ investment orders, including but not limited to money market, securities, investment funds and FX transactions. Confirm with / advise clients on their order status in a timely manner. From time to time go through the selling process, suitability check, key economic factors and main risk factors of certain products with clients when needed.

- Liaises and coordinates with Credit Department to ensure all clients’ credit applications are properly processed.

- Attends internal / external trainings / meetings when possible and required. Ensuring fit and proper requirements (such as CPT requirement, AML training, fraud training and compliance certification etc.) as stipulated by the regulatory are completely met.

- General ad-hoc duties such as to perform routine follow up and administration work.

- Assists to ad-hoc IT projects on enhancement or upgrade of current IT systems if required

- Back up other Assistant Relationship Managers as and when required.

## Qualifications

- University graduate

- Preferably with 2-3 years of private banking experience

- Fluent in English, Cantonese and Mandarin

- Professional qualifications such as Enhanced Competency Framework (ECF) would be an advantage.

- The employment is subject to Mandatory Reference Checking Scheme (MRCS) as per regulatory requirement. For details, please refer to Hong Kong Monetary Authority - Mandatory Reference Checking Scheme

- Liaises and coordinates with Credit Department to ensure all clients’ credit applications are properly processed.

- Attends internal / external trainings / meetings when possible and required. Ensuring fit and proper requirements (such as CPT requirement, AML training, fraud training and compliance certification etc.) as stipulated by the regulatory are completely met.

- General ad-hoc duties such as to perform routine follow up and administration work.

- Assists to ad-hoc IT projects on enhancement or upgrade of current IT systems if required

- Back up other Assistant Relationship Managers as and when required.

## Qualifications

- University graduate

- Preferably with 2-3 years of private banking experience

- Fluent in English, Cantonese and Mandarin

- Professional qualifications such as Enhanced Competency Framework (ECF) would be an advantage.

- The employment is subject to Mandatory Reference Checking Scheme (MRCS) as per regulatory requirement. For details, please refer to Hong Kong Monetary Authority - Mandatory Reference Checking Scheme
descriptionByLocale.de:
## Main Duties & Responsibilities

- Introduces the Bank and its banking services to prospective clients.

- Provides support to Relationship Manager to achieve their business goals such as NNA / Revenue targets.

- Markets and provides comprehensive private banking services to meet varied wealth management needs.

- Performs KYC / AML due diligence on clients in compliance with the Bank’s general directive and ongoing periodic review from time to time.

- Go through the Investment Risk Profile Questionnaire with clients at time of account opening as well as ongoing periodic review to ensure and update clients’ investment product suitability, risk tolerance and investment experience from time to time.

- Liaises and coordinates with the Bank’s investment units, e.g. Treasury Department and Global Product Unit for passing on product information and markets commentary to clients from time to time.

- Closely monitor clients’ portfolio and account activities to ensure the compliance with internal guidelines as well as external regulations from time to time. Monitor clients’ position to ensure collateral sufficiency at all times and prompt regularization of excesses, if any.

- Prepares and maintains proper records including but not limited to account documentation, CIP, IRPQ, credit application and relevant documents, trade transactions and call reports, etc.

- Accept, confirm and execute clients’ investment orders, including but not limited to money market, securities, investment funds and FX transactions. Confirm with / advise clients on their order status in a timely manner. From time to time go through the selling process, suitability check, key economic factors and main risk factors of certain products with clients when needed.

- Liaises and coordinates with Credit Department to ensure all clients’ credit applications are properly processed.

- Attends internal / external trainings / meetings when possible and required. Ensuring fit and proper requirements (such as CPT requirement, AML training, fraud training and compliance certification etc.) as stipulated by the regulatory are completely met.

- General ad-hoc duties such as to perform routine follow up and administration work.

- Assists to ad-hoc IT projects on enhancement or upgrade of current IT systems if required

- Back up other Assistant Relationship Managers as and when required.

## Qualifications

- University graduate

- Preferably with 2-3 years of private banking experience

- Fluent in English, Cantonese and Mandarin

- Professional qualifications such as Enhanced Competency Framework (ECF) would be an advantage.

- The employment is subject to Mandatory Reference Checking Scheme (MRCS) as per regulatory requirement. For details, please refer to Hong Kong Monetary Authority - Mandatory Reference Checking Scheme

jobs.title: Client Service Officer
jobs.description:
## Key Responsibilities

- Receive, prepare and initiate the execution of customer’s instructions complying with internal procedures (authorizations, establishment of internal forms, collecting of internal signatures if required, transmitting the instructions to the relevant department).

- Manage investment instructions received by the Clients.

- Collect customer’s instructions in case of corporate action events.

- Follow-up on the accuracy and the completeness of the customers files.

- Follow-up on pending issues and escalate to the Customer Relationship Officer (CRO) if necessary.

- Be the contact person on behalf of the team for the other departments of the bank whenever needed (troubleshooting, requests, etc.).

- Be the alternative contact person to the Client Relationship Officer for customers (phone answering, meetings, etc.).

- Perform research work upon CRO’s request.

- Prepare any documental support on behalf of the CRO.

- Assist the CRO in any other delegated tasks.

- Monitor Credit on a daily or monthly basis (limit excess review, collateral excess review, overdrafts, risk concentration, significant loss, etc.).

- Monitor Operation quality on a daily basis (deposit renewal, pending orders, MiFID profile, negative cash balance check, etc.).

- Monitor Risk on a yearly basis (dormant accounts, hold mail account, small balance accounts, claims escalation and follow up on a case by case basis, etc.).

## Skills and experience

## Experience: Minimum of 3 years in a similar role within private banking or financial services.

Knowledge: Strong understanding of private banking processes, financial products, and regulatory requirements (KYC, AML, FATCA, CRS).

Languages: Excellent proficiency in French and, English, Italian is considered as asset.

Technical Skills: Proficient in standard IT systems; ability to quickly learn new software.

Personal Attributes

- Service-oriented and client-focused mindset

- Strong team player with a collaborative spirit

- High level of confidentiality and discretion

- Proactive and self-motivated

- Detail-oriented, accurate, and reliable

- Open-minded and eager to learn new skills

Our Values

- Accountability: Taking ownership for tasks and challenges, as well as seeking continuous improvement.

- Hands-on: Being proactive to rapidly deliver high-quality results.

- Passionate: Being committed and striving for excellence.

- Solution-driven: Focusing on client outcomes and treating clients fairly with a risk-aware mindset.

- Partnership-oriented: Promoting collaboration and teamwork. Working together with an entrepreneurial spirit.

descriptionByLocale.it:
Responsabilità chiave
- Ricevere, preparare e avviare l'esecuzione delle istruzioni del cliente in conformità con le procedure interne (autorizzazioni, creazione di moduli interni, raccolta di firme interne, se necessario, trasmettendo le istruzioni al reparto interessato).
- Gestire le istruzioni di investimento ricevute dai Clienti.
- Raccogliere le istruzioni del cliente in caso di eventi di azione aziendale.
- Seguire l'accuratezza e la completezza dei file dei clienti. - follow-up su questioni in sospeso ed escalate al Customer Relationship Officer (CRO) se necessario.
- No. Essere la persona di contatto per conto del team per gli altri dipartimenti della banca ogni volta che necessario (risoluzione dei problemi, richieste, ecc.)
- Siate la persona di contatto alternativa al Responsabile relazioni cliente per i clienti (risposte telefoniche, riunioni, ecc.).
- Eseguire i lavori di ricerca su richiesta del CRO.
- Preparare qualsiasi supporto documentale a nome del CRO.
- Assistere il CRO in qualsiasi altro compito delegato.
- Monitorare il credito su base giornaliera o mensile (limite di revisione in eccesso, revisione in eccesso collaterale, overdraft, concentrazione di rischio, perdita significativa, ecc.).
- Monitorare la qualità dell'operazione su base giornaliera (rinnovamento deposito, ordini in sospeso, profilo MiFID, controllo saldo negativo, ecc.).
- Monitorare il rischio su base annua (conti di manutenzione, tenere il conto della posta, conti di piccola bilancia, rivendica l'escalation e seguire su un caso per caso, ecc.).

## Abilità ed esperienza

## Esperienza: Minimo di 3 anni in un ruolo simile all'interno di servizi bancari privati o finanziari. Conoscenza: Forte comprensione dei processi bancari privati, dei prodotti finanziari e dei requisiti normativi (KYC, AML, FATCA, CRS). Lingue: Ottima competenza in francese e, inglese, italiano è considerato come risorsa. Competenze tecniche: Proficiente nei sistemi IT standard; capacità di imparare rapidamente nuovo software. Personal Attributes
- Mensetto orientato al servizio e orientato al cliente
- Giocatore di squadra forte con uno spirito collaborativo
- Alto livello di riservatezza e discrezione
- Proattivo e auto-motivato
- Dettaglio orientato, accurato e affidabile
- Aperto e desideroso di imparare nuove competenze I nostri valori
- Contabilità: Prendere la proprietà per compiti e sfide, così come la ricerca di miglioramento continuo.
- Hands-on: essere proattivi per fornire rapidamente risultati di alta qualità.
- Passionato: Impegnarsi e lottare per l'eccellenza.
- Soluzione-driven: Focusing sui risultati del cliente e il trattamento dei clienti abbastanza con una mentalità di rischio-consapevole.
- Partnership-oriented: promuovere la collaborazione e il lavoro di squadra. Lavorare insieme a uno spirito imprenditoriale.
descriptionByLocale.de:
Key Responsibilities
- Empfangen, bereiten und initiieren Sie die Ausführung der Anweisungen des Kunden, die internen Verfahren (Berechtigungen, Einrichtung von internen Formularen, Sammeln von internen Signaturen, falls erforderlich, Übermittlung der Anweisungen an die betreffende Abteilung).
- Verwalten Sie Investitionsanweisungen von den Kunden erhalten.
- Sammeln Sie die Anweisungen des Kunden im Falle von Aktionsveranstaltungen.
- Folge der Genauigkeit und der Vollständigkeit der Kundendateien.
- Follow-up zu anstehenden Fragen und eskalieren Sie bei Bedarf an den Customer Relationship Officer (CRO).
- Ja. Seien Sie die Kontaktperson im Namen des Teams für die anderen Abteilungen der Bank, wenn nötig (Troubleshooting, Anfragen, etc.). Seien Sie die alternative Kontaktperson zum Client Relationship Officer für Kunden (Telefonbeantwortung, Treffen, etc.).
- Führen Sie Forschungsarbeiten auf CROs Anfrage durch.
- Bereiten Sie alle dokumentarischen Unterstützung im Namen der KRO vor.
- Überwachen Sie die Kredite täglich oder monatlich (beschränken Sie die Überschreitung der Sicherheiten, Überziehungen, Überziehungen, Risikokonzentration, signifikante Verluste usw.).
- Überwachung der Betriebsqualität auf einer täglichen Basis (Entzugserneuerung, anhängige Bestellungen, MiFID-Profil, Negativ-Cash-Balance-Check, etc.).
- Überwachen Sie das Risiko auf jährlicher Basis (Lieferkonten, halten Sie E-Mail-Konto, kleine Bilanzkonten, Forderungen Eskalation und folgen Sie auf einem Fall nach Fall, etc.). Erfahrung und Erfahrung: Mindestens 3 Jahre in einer ähnlichen Rolle innerhalb privater Banken oder Finanzdienstleistungen. Wissen: Starkes Verständnis von Privatbankprozessen, Finanzprodukten und regulatorischen Anforderungen (KYC, AML, FATCA, CRS). Sprachen: Hervorragende Sprachkenntnisse in Französisch und Englisch, Italienisch gilt als Asset. Technische Fähigkeiten: Proficient in Standard-IT-Systemen; Fähigkeit, schnell neue Software zu lernen. Persönliche Attribute
- Service-orientiertes und kundenorientiertes Mindset
- Starker Teamplayer mit einem kollaborativen Geist
- Hohe Vertraulichkeit und Diskretion
- Proaktive und selbstmotivierte
- Detail-orientiert, genau und zuverlässig
- Offen-gesinnt und eifrig, neue Fähigkeiten zu lernen Unsere Werte
- Rechenschaftspflicht: Eigentum an Aufgaben und Herausforderungen zu nehmen, sowie kontinuierliche Verbesserung zu suchen.
- Hands-on: Als proaktiv, schnell qualitativ hochwertige Ergebnisse zu liefern.
- Leidenschaftlich: Sein Engagement und Streben nach Exzellenz.
- Lösungsgetrieben: Konzentrieren Sie sich auf Client-Ergebnisse und Behandlung von Clients ziemlich mit einem risikobewussten Mindset.
- Partnerschaftsorientiert: Förderung von Zusammenarbeit und Teamarbeit. Zusammenarbeit mit einem Unternehmergeist.
descriptionByLocale.fr:
Principales responsabilités
- Recevoir, préparer et commencer l'exécution des instructions du client se conformant aux procédures internes (autorisations, établissement de formulaires internes, collecte de signatures internes au besoin, transmission des instructions au ministère concerné).
- Gérer les instructions d'investissement reçues par les clients.
- Recueillir les instructions du client en cas d'actions d'entreprise.
- Suivi de l'exactitude et de l'exhaustivité des dossiers des clients.
- Suivi des questions en suspens et passage au poste de responsable des relations avec la clientèle (CRO) si nécessaire.
- Oui. Être la personne-ressource au nom de l'équipe pour les autres services de la banque chaque fois que nécessaire (dépannage, demandes, etc.). Soyez l'autre personne-ressource de l'agente relation client pour les clients (réponse téléphonique, réunions, etc.).
- Effectuer des recherches sur demande du CRO.
- Préparer tout soutien documentaire au nom du CRO.
- Aider le CRO dans toute autre tâche déléguée.
- Surveiller le crédit tous les jours ou tous les mois (réexamen de l'excédent des limites, examen de l'excédent des garanties, découverts, concentration des risques, perte importante, etc.).
- Surveiller quotidiennement la qualité de l'opération (renouvellement du dépôt, commandes en attente, profil MiFID, contrôle négatif du solde de trésorerie, etc.).
- Surveiller le risque sur une base annuelle (comptes dormants, comptes de dépôt, comptes de petits soldes, hausse des créances et suivi au cas par cas, etc.).

## Compétences et expérience

## Expérience : minimum de 3 ans dans un rôle similaire au sein des services bancaires ou financiers privés. Connaissance : Bonne compréhension des processus bancaires privés, des produits financiers et des exigences réglementaires (KYC, AML, FATCA, CRS). Langues: Excellente compétence en français et, anglais, italien est considéré comme un atout. Compétences techniques : Connaissance des systèmes informatiques standard; aptitude à apprendre rapidement de nouveaux logiciels. Attributs personnels
- Orientation vers le service et orientation vers le client
- Solide joueur d'équipe avec un esprit de collaboration
- Niveau élevé de confidentialité et de discrétion
- Proactive et automotivée
- Orientation vers le détail, précision et fiabilité
- Ouvert et désireux d'acquérir de nouvelles compétences Nos valeurs
- Responsabilité : Prendre en main les tâches et les défis, et chercher à améliorer continuellement.
- Hands-on: être proactif pour obtenir rapidement des résultats de haute qualité.
- Passionné : être engagé et viser l'excellence.
- La solution est axée sur les résultats des clients et le traitement équitable des clients avec un état d'esprit conscient des risques.
- Objectif partenariat : promouvoir la collaboration et le travail d'équipe. Travailler ensemble avec un esprit d'entreprise.

jobs.title: Fund Services, Assistant Manager
jobs.description:
## Job Description

The role requires you to support the Director and Senior Managers in the efficient and effective functioning of the team, providing day to day support and contributing to the effective execution of team objectives, whilst developing the skills necessary for future progression into full managerial responsibilities.

Working closely with senior leadership and cross functional teams, you will help to ensure that processes run smoothly, compliance standards are met, and service levels are maintained.

This role supports continuous improvement initiatives, assists in managing workloads and priorities, and contributes to the delivery of high-quality services that enable the business to operate efficiently and effectively.

## Main Responsibilities

- Perform your role in accordance with established procedures, taking the lead on day-to-day activities and assuming ownership for compliance and fund governance aspects of the services provided to clients, with limited supervision.

- Conduct compliance testing and monitoring as part of the Compliance Monitoring Programmes for regulated fund structures, ensuring all findings are addressed and documented.

- Prepare six-monthly compliance reports for client entities, presenting key findings and updates for board meetings.

- Oversee the client periodic review process for Funds Services, including monitoring and clearing external audit points raised for fund clients.

- Develop, maintain, and enhance systems, controls, policies, and procedures to ensure adherence to Group directives, regulatory requirements, and evolving industry standards.

- Demonstrate a solid understanding of different regulated fund types and the governance requirements associated with each, supporting the team in delivering high-quality service.

- Proactively manage operational and fiduciary risks, independently organising and prioritising tasks to ensure timely and effective service delivery.

- Supervise and support junior colleagues, monitoring workflow, delegating tasks, and acting as a point of escalation for compliance-related queries.

- Contribute to projects and change initiatives, continually identifying opportunities for improvement, standardisation, and increased efficiency in fund governance and compliance processes.

- Maintain compliance with all relevant laws, regulations, codes of practice, and internal policies; assess and escalate risks to management as appropriate.

- Take responsibility for your own professional development, ensuring all CPD and relevant training requirements are met annually.

## Skills and experience

- Recognised Professional Qualification - e.g. Accountancy, Chartered Secretary, STEP, Banking, Securities, Compliance or other relevant Table 5 qualification (working towards Table 5) along with relevant experience in AML/CFT/CPF and Financial Regulation.

- 3 years' relevant finance industry experience working within an offshore financial services organisation, with a good understanding of all areas of client administration and fiduciary services.

- A good knowledge and understanding of the local finance industry, legislation and regulatory requirements in the environment in which we operate.

- Possess a strong commitment to client service excellence

- Ability to manage workloads effectively whilst navigating varying deadlines.

- Ability to work accurately under pressure, with minimum supervision and communicate with clarity and purpose both in written and verbal forms.

- Strong interpersonal skills are essential.

- Proficient in the use of a range of Microsoft products (Outlook, Word, Excel and PowerPoint).  Knowledge of PureClient, FrontInvest and/or Navision is desirable.

## Our Values

-  Accountability : Taking ownership for tasks and challenges, as well as seeking continuous improvement

-  Hands-on : Being proactive to rapidly deliver high-quality results

-  Passionate : Being committed and striving for excellence

-  Solution-driven : Focusing on client outcomes and treating clients fairly with a risk-aware mindset

-  Partnership-oriented : Promoting collaboration and teamwork. Working together with an entrepreneurial spirit.

- A good knowledge and understanding of the local finance industry, legislation and regulatory requirements in the environment in which we operate.

- Possess a strong commitment to client service excellence

- Ability to manage workloads effectively whilst navigating varying deadlines.

- Ability to work accurately under pressure, with minimum supervision and communicate with clarity and purpose both in written and verbal forms.

- Strong interpersonal skills are essential.

- Proficient in the use of a range of Microsoft products (Outlook, Word, Excel and PowerPoint).  Knowledge of PureClient, FrontInvest and/or Navision is desirable.

## Our Values

-  Accountability : Taking ownership for tasks and challenges, as well as seeking continuous improvement

-  Hands-on : Being proactive to rapidly deliver high-quality results

-  Passionate : Being committed and striving for excellence

-  Solution-driven : Focusing on client outcomes and treating clients fairly with a risk-aware mindset

-  Partnership-oriented : Promoting collaboration and teamwork. Working together with an entrepreneurial spirit.
descriptionByLocale.it:
Descrizione del lavoro Il ruolo richiede di supportare il direttore e i senior manager nel funzionamento efficiente ed efficace del team, fornendo supporto giornaliero e contribuire all'efficace esecuzione degli obiettivi del team, sviluppando le competenze necessarie per la futura progressione in piena responsabilità manageriali. Lavorando a stretto contatto con la leadership senior e cross team funzionali, vi aiuterà a garantire che i processi funzionano senza problemi, gli standard di conformità sono rispettati e i livelli di servizio sono mantenuti. Questo ruolo supporta iniziative di miglioramento continuo, assiste nella gestione dei carichi di lavoro e delle priorità, e contribuisce alla fornitura di servizi di alta qualità che permettono al business di operare in modo efficiente ed efficace.

## Responsabilità Principali
- Esegui il tuo ruolo in conformità con le procedure stabilite, prendendo il comando delle attività quotidiane e assumendo la proprietà per gli aspetti di compliance e di governance del fondo dei servizi forniti ai clienti, con supervisione limitata.
- Condurre i test di conformità e il monitoraggio nell'ambito dei programmi di monitoraggio della conformità per le strutture dei fondi regolamentati, assicurando che tutti i risultati siano affrontati e documentati.
- Preparare rapporti di conformità semestrali per le entità client, presentando i risultati chiave e gli aggiornamenti per le riunioni di bordo. - supervisionare il processo di revisione periodica del cliente per i Servizi dei Fondi, incluso il monitoraggio e la compensazione dei punti di audit esterni sollevati per i clienti dei fondi.
- Sviluppare, mantenere e migliorare i sistemi, i controlli, le politiche e le procedure per garantire l'adesione alle direttive del Gruppo, ai requisiti normativi e agli standard industriali in evoluzione.
- Dimostrare una solida comprensione dei diversi tipi di fondi regolamentati e dei requisiti di governance associati a ciascuno, sostenendo il team nella fornitura di un servizio di alta qualità.
- Gestione attiva dei rischi operativi e fiduciari, organizzazione e priorità in modo indipendente per garantire la consegna tempestiva ed efficace dei servizi.
- Supervisionare e sostenere i colleghi junior, monitorare il flusso di lavoro, delegare i compiti e agire come punto di escalation per le domande relative alla conformità.
- Contribuire a progetti e iniziative di cambiamento, identificare continuamente opportunità di miglioramento, standardizzazione, e aumentare l'efficienza nei processi di governance dei fondi e conformità.
- Mantenere il rispetto di tutte le leggi, regolamenti, codici di pratica e politiche interne; valutare e aumentare i rischi per la gestione come appropriato.
- No. Prendere la responsabilità per il proprio sviluppo professionale, assicurando tutti i requisiti di formazione CPD e pertinenti sono soddisfatti ogni anno.

## Abilità ed esperienza
- Qualificazione Professionale riconosciuta - ad esempio Contabilità, Sottosegretario Chartered, STEP, Banking, Securities, Compliance o altre qualificazioni Tabella 5 rilevanti (lavorando verso la tabella 5) con esperienza rilevante in AML/CFT/CPF e Regolamento Finanziario. - 3 anni di esperienza nel settore finanziario rilevante che lavorano all'interno di un'organizzazione di servizi finanziari offshore, con una buona comprensione di tutte le aree di amministrazione clienti e servizi fiduciari.
- Una buona conoscenza e comprensione del settore finanziario locale, della legislazione e dei requisiti normativi nell'ambiente in cui operiamo.
- Possede un forte impegno per l'eccellenza del servizio clienti
- Capacità di gestire i carichi di lavoro in modo efficace durante la navigazione scadenze variabili.
- Capacità di lavorare con precisione sotto pressione, con minima supervisione e comunicazione con chiarezza e finalità sia in forma scritta che verbale.
- Le forti capacità interpersonali sono essenziali.
- Proficiente nell'uso di una gamma di prodotti Microsoft (Outlook, Word, Excel e PowerPoint). La conoscenza di PureClient, FrontInvest e/o Navision è auspicabile.

## Our Values
- Accountability: Assumere la proprietà per i compiti e le sfide, nonché cercare un miglioramento continuo
- Hands-on: Essere proattivi a fornire rapidamente risultati di alta qualità
- Passionate: Essere impegnati e impegnati per l'eccellenza
- Solution-driven: Focusing sui risultati del cliente e trattare i clienti abbastanza con una mentalità di rischio-aware
- Partnership-oriented: Promuovere la collaborazione e il lavoro di squadra. Lavorare insieme a uno spirito imprenditoriale.
descriptionByLocale.de:
Job Description Die Rolle erfordert, dass Sie den Direktor und Senior Manager bei der effizienten und effektiven Funktion des Teams unterstützen, Tag-zu-Tage-Unterstützung bereitstellen und zur effektiven Umsetzung von Team-Zielen bei der Entwicklung der Fähigkeiten, die für zukünftige Fortschritte in volle Führungsaufgaben erforderlich sind. In enger Zusammenarbeit mit Senior-Führungs- und Cross-Funktionsteams helfen Sie, sicherzustellen, dass Prozesse reibungslos laufen, Compliance-Standards erfüllt werden, und Service-Level werden beibehalten. Diese Rolle unterstützt kontinuierliche Verbesserungsinitiativen, hilft bei der Verwaltung von Arbeitsbelastungen und Prioritäten und trägt zur Erbringung hochwertiger Dienstleistungen bei, die es dem Unternehmen ermöglichen, effizient und effektiv zu arbeiten. Hauptverantwortlichkeiten
- Führen Sie Ihre Rolle in Übereinstimmung mit etablierten Verfahren, führen Sie die Führung an Tag-zu-Tage-Aktivitäten und übernehmen Eigentum für Compliance und Fonds Governance Aspekte der Dienstleistungen, die Kunden zur Verfügung gestellt, mit begrenzter Aufsicht.
- Durchführung von Compliance-Tests und -Überwachung im Rahmen der Compliance-Überwachungsprogramme für regulierte Fondsstrukturen, um sicherzustellen, dass alle Ergebnisse behandelt und dokumentiert werden.
- Bereiten Sie sechsmonatige Compliance-Berichte für Auftraggeber vor, die wichtige Ergebnisse und Aktualisierungen für Vorstandssitzungen darstellen.
- Überwachen Sie den regelmäßigen Überprüfungsprozess des Kunden für Fondsdienstleistungen, einschließlich Überwachung und Clearing externer Auditpunkte für Fondskunden.
- Entwicklung, Aufrechterhaltung und Verbesserung von Systemen, Kontrollen, Richtlinien und Verfahren, um die Einhaltung von Gruppenrichtlinien, Regulierungsanforderungen und die Entwicklung von Industriestandards zu gewährleisten.
- Demonstrieren Sie ein solides Verständnis für verschiedene regulierte Fondstypen und die Governance-Anforderungen, die mit jedem verbunden sind, und unterstützen Sie das Team bei der Bereitstellung hochwertiger Dienstleistungen.
- Beherrschen Sie operative und fiduziäre Risiken, unabhängig von der Organisation und Priorisierung von Aufgaben, um eine rechtzeitige und effektive Service-Lieferung zu gewährleisten.
- Betreuung und Unterstützung von Junior-Kollegen, Überwachung des Workflows, Delegieren von Aufgaben und Handeln als Eskalation für konforme Anfragen.
- Fördern Sie Projekte und ändern Sie Initiativen, um ständig Verbesserungs-, Standardisierungs- und Effizienzsteigerungen bei Fondsführung und Compliance-Prozessen zu ermitteln.
- Bewahren Sie die Einhaltung aller einschlägigen Gesetze, Vorschriften, Verhaltenskodizes und der internen Politiken; bewerten und eskalieren Sie die Risiken für das Management.
- Ja. Verantwortung für Ihre eigene berufliche Entwicklung übernehmen, um sicherzustellen, dass alle CPD und die relevanten Ausbildungsanforderungen jährlich erfüllt werden. Fähigkeiten und Erfahrungen - anerkannte Berufsqualifikation - z.B. Buchhaltung, Chartered Secretary, STEP, Banking, Wertpapiere, Compliance oder andere relevante Tabelle 5 Qualifikationen (Arbeiten nach Tabelle 5) zusammen mit relevanten Erfahrungen in AML/CFT/CPF und Financial Regulation. - 3 Jahre relevante Erfahrung in der Finanzbranche, die innerhalb einer Offshore-Finanzdienstleistungsorganisation arbeitet, mit einem guten Verständnis aller Bereiche der Kundenverwaltung und der Finanzdienstleistungen.
- Ein gutes Wissen und Verständnis der lokalen Finanzindustrie, Gesetzgebung und regulatorischen Anforderungen in der Umwelt, in der wir arbeiten.
- Ermöglicht ein starkes Engagement für Kundendienst-Exzellenz
- Fähigkeit, Workloads effektiv zu verwalten, während Sie verschiedene Termine navigieren.
- Fähigkeit, unter Druck genau zu arbeiten, mit minimaler Aufsicht und Kommunikation mit Klarheit und Zweck sowohl in schriftlichen als auch verbalen Formen.
- Starke zwischenmenschliche Fähigkeiten sind unerlässlich.
- Proficient in der Verwendung einer Reihe von Microsoft-Produkten (Outlook, Word, Excel und PowerPoint). Die Kenntnis von PureClient, FrontInvest und/oder Navision ist wünschenswert. Unsere Werte
- Rechenschaftspflicht : Eigentumsbesitz für Aufgaben und Herausforderungen zu nehmen, sowie kontinuierliche Verbesserung zu suchen
- Hands-on : Being proaktiv, um schnell qualitativ hochwertige Ergebnisse zu liefern
- Passionate: Engagement und Streben nach Exzellenz
- Lösungsgetrieben : Fokussieren auf Kundenergebnisse und Behandlung von Kunden mit einem risikobewussten Mindset
- Partnerschaftsorientierte: Förderung der Zusammenarbeit und Teamarbeit. Zusammenarbeit mit einem Unternehmergeist.
descriptionByLocale.fr:
Description de l'emploi Le rôle exige que vous souteniez le directeur et les cadres supérieurs dans le fonctionnement efficace et efficient de l'équipe, en leur fournissant un soutien quotidien et en contribuant à l'exécution efficace des objectifs de l'équipe, tout en développant les compétences nécessaires à la progression future vers des responsabilités de gestion complètes. En travaillant en étroite collaboration avec les cadres supérieurs et les équipes interfonctionnelles, vous vous assurerez que les processus fonctionnent bien, que les normes de conformité sont respectées et que les niveaux de service sont maintenus. Ce rôle appuie les initiatives d'amélioration continue, aide à gérer la charge de travail et les priorités et contribue à la prestation de services de haute qualité qui permettent à l'entreprise de fonctionner efficacement.

## Principales responsabilités
- Exécutez votre rôle conformément aux procédures établies, dirigez les activités quotidiennes et assumez la propriété des aspects de la conformité et de la gouvernance des services fournis aux clients, avec une supervision limitée.
- Effectuer des essais et des contrôles de conformité dans le cadre des programmes de surveillance de la conformité pour les structures de fonds réglementées, en veillant à ce que toutes les constatations soient prises en compte et documentées.
- Préparer des rapports semestriels sur la conformité pour les entités clientes, en présentant les principales constatations et mises à jour pour les réunions du conseil.
- Superviser le processus d'examen périodique des clients pour les services des fonds, y compris le suivi et l'élimination des points d'audit externe recueillis pour les clients des fonds.
- Élaborer, maintenir et améliorer les systèmes, les contrôles, les politiques et les procédures pour assurer le respect des directives du groupe, des exigences réglementaires et de l'évolution des normes de l'industrie.
- Démontrer une bonne compréhension des différents types de fonds réglementés et des exigences de gouvernance associées à chacun, en aidant l'équipe à fournir des services de haute qualité.
- Gérer de manière proactive les risques opérationnels et fiduciaires, organiser et hiérarchiser les tâches de manière indépendante afin d'assurer une prestation de services rapide et efficace.
- Superviser et soutenir les collègues subalternes, surveiller les flux de travail, déléguer des tâches et agir comme un point d'escalade pour les questions liées à la conformité.
- Contribuer aux projets et aux initiatives de changement, identifier en permanence les possibilités d'amélioration, de normalisation et d'efficacité accrue dans les processus de gouvernance et de conformité des fonds.
- Maintenir le respect de toutes les lois, règlements, codes de pratique et politiques internes pertinents; évaluer et accroître les risques pour la gestion, le cas échéant.
- Oui. Prenez la responsabilité de votre propre perfectionnement professionnel, en veillant à ce que toutes les exigences en matière de DPC et de formation soient respectées chaque année.

## Compétences et expérience
- Qualification professionnelle reconnue - p. ex. comptabilité, secrétaire agréé, STEP, banques, valeurs mobilières, conformité ou autre qualification pertinente du tableau 5 (travaillant vers le tableau 5), ainsi que l'expérience pertinente en matière de LMA/FT/PFC et de réglementation financière. - 3 ans d'expérience pertinente de l'industrie financière travaillant au sein d'une organisation de services financiers offshore, avec une bonne compréhension de tous les domaines de l'administration client et des services fiduciaires.
- Une bonne connaissance et une bonne compréhension de l'industrie financière locale, de la législation et des exigences réglementaires dans l'environnement dans lequel nous opérons.
- Avoir un engagement ferme envers l'excellence du service à la clientèle
- Capacité de gérer efficacement la charge de travail tout en naviguant dans des délais variables.
- Capacité de travailler avec précision sous pression, avec une supervision minimale et de communiquer avec clarté et finalité, tant par écrit que verbalement.
- De fortes aptitudes interpersonnelles sont essentielles.
- Compétence dans l'utilisation d'une gamme de produits Microsoft (Outlook, Word, Excel et PowerPoint). La connaissance de PureClient, FrontInvest et/ou Navision est souhaitable.

## Nos valeurs
- Responsabilité : S'approprier les tâches et les défis, ainsi que chercher une amélioration continue
- Hands-on : Être proactif pour obtenir rapidement des résultats de haute qualité
- Passionné : S'engager et s'efforcer d'atteindre l'excellence
- Inspiré par la solution : Se concentrer sur les résultats des clients et traiter les clients équitablement avec un état d'esprit conscient des risques
- Orienté par le partenariat : Promouvoir la collaboration et le travail d'équipe. Travailler ensemble avec un esprit d'entreprise.

summary: The role requires you to support the Director and Senior Managers in the efficient and effective functioning of the team, providing day to day support and contributing to the effective execution of team objectives, whilst developing the skills necessary for future progression into full managerial responsibilities.
Working closely with senior leadership and cross functional teams, you will help to ensure that processes run smoothly, compliance standards are met, and service levels are maintained.

This role supports continuous improvement initiatives, assists in managing workloads and priorities, and contributes to the delivery of high-quality services that enable the business to operate efficiently and effectively.
responsibilities: Perform your role in accordance with established procedures, taking the lead on day-to-day activities and assuming ownership for compliance and fund governance aspects of the services provided to clients, with limited supervision.
Conduct compliance testing and monitoring as part of the Compliance Monitoring Programmes for regulated fund structures, ensuring all findings are addressed and documented.
Prepare six-monthly compliance reports for client entities, presenting key findings and updates for board meetings.
Oversee the client periodic review process for Funds Services, including monitoring and clearing external audit points raised for fund clients.
Develop, maintain, and enhance systems, controls, policies, and procedures to ensure adherence to Group directives, regulatory requirements, and evolving industry standards.
Demonstrate a solid understanding of different regulated fund types and the governance requirements associated with each, supporting the team in delivering high-quality service.
Proactively manage operational and fiduciary risks, independently organising and prioritising tasks to ensure timely and effective service delivery.
Supervise and support junior colleagues, monitoring workflow, delegating tasks, and acting as a point of escalation for compliance-related queries.
Contribute to projects and change initiatives, continually identifying opportunities for improvement, standardisation, and increased efficiency in fund governance and compliance processes.
Maintain compliance with all relevant laws, regulations, codes of practice, and internal policies; assess and escalate risks to management as appropriate.
Take responsibility for your own professional development, ensuring all CPD and relevant training requirements are met annually.

sections.heading: Our Company
paragraphs: EFG International is a global private banking group, offering private banking and asset management services. We serve clients in over 40 locations worldwide. EFG International offers a stimulating and dynamic work environment and strives to be an employer of choice.

EFG is committed to providing an equitable and inclusive working environment that is founded on the principle of mutual respect. Joining our team means experiencing a supportive environment, where your contributions are valued and recognised. We strongly believe that the diversity of our teams gives us a competitive advantage by fostering better decision-making and greater innovation.

sections.heading: Our Purpose and Mission
paragraphs: Empowering entrepreneurial minds to create value – today and for the future.

We are a private bank, offering personalised solutions on a global scale to private and institutional clients. Our sustainable success is based on our talents and on how we partner with our clients and communities to create lasting value.

jobs.title: Associate Director, Client Onboarding & Periodic Review (Contract)
jobs.description:
## Main Duties & Responsibilities

- According to the regulatory requirements and internal policies, perform CDD/KYC review on Client Profile, SOW plausibility, transactions review and screenings for client onboarding. periodic review and triggered review.

- Ensure that the CDD information (covering documents, account structure, PEP status, adverse news, system data, tax related papers, etc) obtained during the CDD process up-to-date and relevant.

- Identify and assess any risk during review and escalate to compliance if necessary.

- Follow ups with CRO/CSO to request for supplementary documentation and ensure timely completion of Account Opening, Periodic Review and Triggered Reviews.

- Acting as Case manager, tracking and reporting the status of Account Opening/Periodic Review and Triggered Reviews to Head of PBS.

- Facilitate the case discussions with Front Office and compliance.

- Participates in and contributes to ad hoc initiatives as well as special projects

## Qualification Requirements

•    Bachelor degree or above in Business Administration, Finance, Economics, or related field;

 •    Sound knowledge in AML / KYC risks with at least 5-6 years relevant experience, preferable in Private Banking;

 •    Demonstrated ability to multitask and work with minimal supervision, adapt and adjust to changing work priorities.

Ensure that the CDD information (covering documents, account structure, PEP status, adverse news, system data, tax related papers, etc) obtained during the CDD process up-to-date and relevant.
Identify and assess any risk during review and escalate to compliance if necessary.

jobs.title: Client Relationship Officer
jobs.description:
Main Duties & Responsibilities

- Risk Management and Compliance:
- Adherence to laws and regulations, internal risk and compliance policies and the Group Code of Conduct.

- Completion of all mandatory risk and compliance training including e-learning within stated timeframe.

- Proactive identification of risks and concerns, including escalation to all relevant stakeholders.

- Timely mitigation of risks and resolution of issues including collaboration with all relevant stakeholder functions and establishment of effective controls.

- Introduces the Bank and its banking services to prospective clients.

- Supports the expected goal of business performance set forth by the management.

- Markets and provides private banking services to clients including but not limited to banking services, investment services, wealth planning and trust, etc.

- Performs KYC / AML due diligence on clients in compliance with the Bank’s general directive during the course of account opening process and ongoing periodic review from time to time.

- Goes through the Investment Risk Profile Questionnaire with clients at time of account opening as well as performs ongoing periodic review to ensure clients’ investment product suitability, risk tolerant and investment experience from time to time.

- Liaises and coordinates with the Bank’s investment units, e.g. Treasury Department, EFG Asset Management, Global Product Unit for passing on product information and market commentary to clients from time to time.

- Closely monitors clients’ portfolio and account activities to ensure the compliance with internal guidelines as well as external regulations from time to time. Monitors clients’ position to ensure client acknowledge the MTM loss if over 20% of portfolio or USD500,000, collateral sufficiency at all times and prompt regularization of excesses, if any.

- Prepares and maintains proper records including but not limited to account documentation, CIP, credit application and relevant documents, trade transactions and call reports, etc.

- Responsibly and effectively manages business expenses.

Qualifications Requirements

- Licenses: Type 1 and Type 4.

- Certificate – CPWP.

- University degree or equivalent

Professional qualifications such as Enhanced Competency Framework (ECF) would be an advantage.

The employment is subject to Mandatory Reference Checking Scheme (MRCS) as per regulatory requirement. For details, please refer to Hong Kong Monetary Authority - Mandatory Reference Checking Scheme

jobs.title: Business Risk Manager - PB COO
jobs.description:
## Key Responsibilities

- Develop and maintain a comprehensive risk and control assessment for all aspects of risk within the FLoD, including effective oversight of the testing programme.

- Execute   a risk-based control monitoring programme   across the function, evidencing the adequacy and effectiveness of key controls.

- Perform regular business controls and monitoring on areas such as Investment Suitability, Cross Border, Anti-Fraud, etc., including management reporting and proactive recommendations.

- Conduct thematic reviews, investigations and gap analyses across FLoD activities.

- Participate in and/or lead projects and change initiatives to implement process and control enhancements.

- Promote a strong risk awareness and client fairness culture across the business.

- Collaborate closely with Second Line of Defense, Global Functions and other internal stakeholders to identify, assess and mitigate risks and ensure appropriate controls are in place.

- Proactively escalate and report issues, provide advice and challenge existing processes related to business activities.

- Manage the FLoD Risk Scorecard, ensuring proper tracking, execution and data integrity.

- Provide professional advice to front office staff on investment advisory activities to ensure compliance with internal policies, procedures and local regulatory requirements.

- Build strong relationships and credibility with key internal stakeholders.

- Provide guidance to junior team members and new joiners,  and act as a checker to ensure quality and accuracy of reviews.

- Undertake any other duties within Business Risk Management as directed by the Head of Business Risk Management

## Role Requirement

-  5+ years and solid business risk management experience in private banking / wealth management.

-  Strong analytical skills, sound judgement and a high level of integrity and rule compliance.

-  Ability to work independently, challenge the status quo and build strong stakeholder relationships

jobs.title: DataOps Engineer
jobs.description:
General Info

- Department: Data Ops Engineer

- Work time Percentage: 100%

- Location: Singapore

Our Company

EFG International is a global private banking group, offering private banking and asset management services. We serve clients in over 40 locations worldwide. EFG International offers a stimulating and dynamic work environment and strives to be an employer of choice.

Our Purpose and Mission

Empowering entrepreneurial minds to create value – today and for the future.

Job Description

## Introduction to the Team

- DataOps Team is responsible for seemless integration, release and monitoring of mission critical data pipelines and workflows

- Currently based in Geneva, we are expanding our global footprint by establishing a virtual team presence in Singapore.

- Reporting to the local IT Head of Applications, the role will be a key pillar in our follow-the-sun support model, ensuring continuous operational excellence across Asia and Europe timezone.

- Our data platform is evolving into a more converged stack, unifying data streams and batches. We are expanding into a cloud based lakehouse and a microservice based data streaming integration model supporting business critical integrations

- To support team activity Python, SQL and CI/CD are essentialskills. This role requires a rigorous approach to documentation and procedure development.

- The team is responsible for building, testing, deploying and monitor the operating part of the platform.

Tasks and Responsibilities

- Take operational ownership of specific data services (change management, quality assurance, SLA, SOP)

- Track and ensure operations continuity of critical data pipelines across the group during Asian hours, adopting a follow-the sun support model.

- Design, implement, and optimize data pipeline in batch and streaming mode.

- Follow and apply CI/CD practice to automate and streamline deployments and integrations.

- Create, maintain, improve documentation and operational procedures as part of operational ownership

- Collaborate with cross-functional teams to review governance and improve integration operations.

- Maintain high standards of data quality, reliability, and performance.

- Stay current with advancements in data engineering, of dataops tech stack, to support continuous improvement.

Requirements and Qualifications

- Experience in data engineering and data operations roles.

- Proven hands-on experience with SQL, Python, PySpark and Databricks

- Knowledge of micro-services architecture, API management and observability is highly desirable.

- Solid understanding of CI/CD platforms and processes (e.g., Azure DevOps, GitLabCI, GitHub Actions).

- Experience with cloud data platforms (Azure preferred).

- Demonstrated ability to produce rigorous, clear, and comprehensive documentation and procedures.

- Familiarity with database design.

- Excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail.

- Effective communication and collaboration skills.

Personal Attributes

- Responsible and reliable, especially during critical operational periods

- Rigorous and methodical in documentation and process creation

- Motivated and goal-oriented

- Curious, enthusiastic, creative, and proactive

- Passionate about analytics and technology, with a strong desire to learn and grow

- Fluent in spoken and written English

Our Values

Please ensure to attach a cover letter to your CV when filling the application.

Application

Should you wish to apply for this position use this link to apply.

EFG International is a global private banking group, offering private banking and asset management services. We serve clients in over 40 locations worldwide. EFG International offers a stimulating and dynamic work environment and strives to be an employer of choice.

Our Purpose and Mission

Empowering entrepreneurial minds to create value – today and for the future.

Job Description

## Introduction to the Team

- DataOps Team is responsible for seemless integration, release and monitoring of mission critical data pipelines and workflows

- Currently based in Geneva, we are expanding our global footprint by establishing a virtual team presence in Singapore.

- Reporting to the local IT Head of Applications, the role will be a key pillar in our follow-the-sun support model, ensuring continuous operational excellence across Asia and Europe timezone.

- Our data platform is evolving into a more converged stack, unifying data streams and batches. We are expanding into a cloud based lakehouse and a microservice based data streaming integration model supporting business critical integrations

- To support team activity Python, SQL and CI/CD are essentialskills. This role requires a rigorous approach to documentation and procedure development.

- The team is responsible for building, testing, deploying and monitor the operating part of the platform.

Tasks and Responsibilities

- Take operational ownership of specific data services (change management, quality assurance, SLA, SOP)

- Track and ensure operations continuity of critical data pipelines across the group during Asian hours, adopting a follow-the sun support model.

- Design, implement, and optimize data pipeline in batch and streaming mode.

- Follow and apply CI/CD practice to automate and streamline deployments and integrations.

- Create, maintain, improve documentation and operational procedures as part of operational ownership

- Collaborate with cross-functional teams to review governance and improve integration operations.

- Maintain high standards of data quality, reliability, and performance.

- Stay current with advancements in data engineering, of dataops tech stack, to support continuous improvement.

Requirements and Qualifications

- Experience in data engineering and data operations roles.

- Proven hands-on experience with SQL, Python, PySpark and Databricks

- Knowledge of micro-services architecture, API management and observability is highly desirable.

- Solid understanding of CI/CD platforms and processes (e.g., Azure DevOps, GitLabCI, GitHub Actions).

- Experience with cloud data platforms (Azure preferred).

- Demonstrated ability to produce rigorous, clear, and comprehensive documentation and procedures.

- Familiarity with database design.

- Excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail.

- Effective communication and collaboration skills.

Personal Attributes

- Responsible and reliable, especially during critical operational periods

- Rigorous and methodical in documentation and process creation

- Motivated and goal-oriented

- Curious, enthusiastic, creative, and proactive

- Passionate about analytics and technology, with a strong desire to learn and grow

- Fluent in spoken and written English

Our Values

Please ensure to attach a cover letter to your CV when filling the application.

Application

Should you wish to apply for this position use this link to apply.

jobs.title: Information Security & BCM Head (Asia)
jobs.description:
General Info
- Department: Global CISO
- Work time Percentage: 100%
- Location: Singapore ideally or Hong-Kong

Our Company
EFG International is a global private banking group, offering private banking and asset management services. We serve clients in over 40 locations worldwide. EFG International offers a stimulating and dynamic work environment and strives to be an employer of choice.

Our Purpose and Mission
Empowering entrepreneurial minds to create value – today and for the future.

Job Description
EFG’s Information Security and BCM team, led by the Group CISO, sets and coordinates global information security strategy, initiatives, and standards across EFG International. The team conducts security risk assessments, manages vulnerability and threat programs, and owns security awareness and training. It safeguards EFG’s infrastructure, applications, and data against breaches, malware, third‑party risks, and cyber-attacks. Additionally, it strengthens the bank’s defence through robust Business Continuity Management, supporting operational resilience and coordinated incident response

The Information Security & BCM Head Asia reports to the Regional COO with a functional line to the Group CISO, and will (i) identify regional risks, threats, and vulnerabilities across the SG and HK branches; (ii) address gaps against local laws, regulations, and industry practices; (iii) develop and tailor information security controls to the region’s risks, adapt policies and procedures to local culture and laws, and facilitate Group strategic initiatives; and (iv) ensure adherence to global Business Continuity Management policies while adopting BCM best practices per local regulatory requirements.

Main responsibilities

Strategy, governance, and leadership

-
Support and implement EFG’s information security strategy and programs, ensuring alignment with local business objectives.

-
Provide regional leadership on information security across SG and HK; serve as the Singapore and Hong Kong Information Security Officer.

-
Manage information security programs directed by Head Office and act as the primary contact for regulatory inquiries, reviews, and inspections.

-
Stay current on regulatory changes and industry trends; perform gap analyses on regulatory papers, circulars, and directives, and recommend improvements.

Risk management, compliance, and audit

-
Identify, evaluate, and manage regional information security risks and vulnerabilities; ensure preventative actions close risk gaps.

-
Conduct regular security assessments and audits to ensure compliance with internal, regulatory, and industry requirements; ensure timely closure of audit items.

-
Develop and maintain local security policies, procedures, and standards aligned with Head Office and regulators.

-
Collaborate on Operational Resilience and Business Continuity (BCP); act as local BCM referant ensuring internal and external requirements (e.g., HKMA OR-2) are met.

Incident management and monitoring

-
Lead and coordinate incident response locally and regionally in close collaboration with the Head Office CISRT, including investigation, root cause analysis, and treatment plans.

-
Review data security breaches, implement remediation, and design and lead the annual cyber-security incident drill for SG & HK.

-
Collaborate with Group Information Security to deploy monitoring tools safeguarding bank information.

Access governance and secure operations

-
Oversee timely completion of local and regional user access recertifications.

-
Approve and periodically review privileged access for business and IT users; recertify IT administrator and technical account access on locally and regionally managed infrastructure.

-
Serve as gatekeeper for data extraction from secure zones.

Stakeholder engagement, communications, and training

-
Build and maintain relationships with stakeholders, clients, leadership, regulators, and Head Office teams.

-
Monitor and manage security communications from Head Office and regulators.

-
Lead security awareness and training programs for employees and relevant stakeholders.

Technology, assessments, and continuous improvement

-
Support assessments for infrastructure and applications; perform periodic security reviews and audits.

-
Recommend enhancements to controls and processes based on emerging threats and best practices.

Reporting, budgeting, and resource management

-
Report timely status of projects, programs, risks, events, and incidents to the Group CISO.

-
Prepare spending proposals; manage local security budgets and resources effectively.

Skills and experience
- Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field.
- 10+ years in information security with strong knowledge of security principles, practices, and technologies.
- Proven experience designing and implementing enterprise security programs.
- Hands-on incident management and incident response expertise.
- Demonstrated leadership, including managing and influencing virtual/remote teams.
- Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills.
- Successful track record working with regulators and regulatory bodies.
- Experience in ICT Risk Management and Business Continuity.
- Industry certifications (e.g., CISSP, CISM, CRISC) preferred.

Our Values
 Accountability: Taking ownership for tasks and challenges, as well as seeking continuous improvement
 Hands-on: Being proactive to rapidly deliver high-quality results
 Passionate: Being committed and striving for excellence
 Solution-driven: Focusing on client outcomes and treating clients fairly with a risk-aware mindset
 Partnership-oriented: Promoting collaboration and teamwork. Working together with an entrepreneurial spirit.

Please ensure to attach a cover letter to your CV when filling the application.
Application
Should you wish to apply for this position use this link to apply.
