Serve as gatekeeper for data extraction from secure zones.

Stakeholder engagement, communications, and training

-
Build and maintain relationships with stakeholders, clients, leadership, regulators, and Head Office teams.

-
Monitor and manage security communications from Head Office and regulators.

-
Lead security awareness and training programs for employees and relevant stakeholders.

Technology, assessments, and continuous improvement

-
Support assessments for infrastructure and applications; perform periodic security reviews and audits.

-
Recommend enhancements to controls and processes based on emerging threats and best practices.

Reporting, budgeting, and resource management

-
Report timely status of projects, programs, risks, events, and incidents to the Group CISO.

-
Prepare spending proposals; manage local security budgets and resources effectively.

Skills and experience
- Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field.
- 10+ years in information security with strong knowledge of security principles, practices, and technologies.
- Proven experience designing and implementing enterprise security programs.
- Hands-on incident management and incident response expertise.
- Demonstrated leadership, including managing and influencing virtual/remote teams.
- Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills.
- Successful track record working with regulators and regulatory bodies.
- Experience in ICT Risk Management and Business Continuity.
- Industry certifications (e.g., CISSP, CISM, CRISC) preferred.

Our Values
 Accountability: Taking ownership for tasks and challenges, as well as seeking continuous improvement
 Hands-on: Being proactive to rapidly deliver high-quality results
 Passionate: Being committed and striving for excellence
 Solution-driven: Focusing on client outcomes and treating clients fairly with a risk-aware mindset
 Partnership-oriented: Promoting collaboration and teamwork. Working together with an entrepreneurial spirit.

Please ensure to attach a cover letter to your CV when filling the application.
Application
Should you wish to apply for this position use this link to apply.

summary:
General Info
- Department: Global CISO
- Work time Percentage: 100%
- Location: Singapore ideally or Hong-Kong


Our Company
EFG International is a global private banking group, offering private banking and asset management services. We serve clients in over 40 locations worldwide. EFG International offers a stimulating and dynamic work environment and strives to be an employer of choice.
EFG is committed to providing an equitable and inclusive working environment that is founded on the principle of mutual respect. Joining our team means experiencing a supportive environment, where your contributions are valued and recognised. We strongly believe that the diversity of our teams gives us a competitive advantage by fostering better decision-making and greater innovation.

Our Purpose and Mission
Empowering entrepreneurial minds to create value – today and for the future.

We are a private bank, offering personalised solutions on a global scale to private and institutional clients. Our sustainable success is based on our talents and on how we partner with our clients and communities to create lasting value.


Job Description
EFG’s Information Security and BCM team, led by the Group CISO, sets and coordinates global information security strategy, initiatives, and standards across EFG International. The team conducts security risk assessments, manages vulnerability and threat programs, and owns security awareness and training. It safeguards EFG’s infrastructure, applications, and data against breaches, malware, third‑party risks, and cyber-attacks. Additionally, it strengthens the bank’s defence through robust Business Continuity Management, supporting operational resilience and coordinated incident response

The Information Security & BCM Head Asia reports to the Regional COO with a functional line to the Group CISO, and will (i) identify regional risks, threats, and vulnerabilities across the SG and HK branches; (ii) address gaps against local laws, regulations, and industry practices; (iii) develop and tailor information security controls to the region’s risks, adapt policies and procedures to local culture and laws, and facilitate Group strategic initiatives; and (iv) ensure adherence to global Business Continuity Management policies while adopting BCM best practices per local regulatory requirements.


Main responsibilities

Strategy, governance, and leadership

  • Support and implement EFG’s information security strategy and programs, ensuring alignment with local business objectives.

    • Provide regional leadership on information security across SG and HK; serve as the Singapore and Hong Kong Information Security Officer.

    • Manage information security programs directed by Head Office and act as the primary contact for regulatory inquiries, reviews, and inspections.

    • Stay current on regulatory changes and industry trends; perform gap analyses on regulatory papers, circulars, and directives, and recommend improvements.

Risk management, compliance, and audit

  • Identify, evaluate, and manage regional information security risks and vulnerabilities; ensure preventative actions close risk gaps.

    • Conduct regular security assessments and audits to ensure compliance with internal, regulatory, and industry requirements; ensure timely closure of audit items.

    • Develop and maintain local security policies, procedures, and standards aligned with Head Office and regulators.

    • Collaborate on Operational Resilience and Business Continuity (BCP); act as local BCM referant ensuring internal and external requirements (e.g., HKMA OR-2) are met.

Incident management and monitoring

  • Lead and coordinate incident response locally and regionally in close collaboration with the Head Office CISRT, including investigation, root cause analysis, and treatment plans.

    • Review data security breaches, implement remediation, and design and lead the annual cyber-security incident drill for SG & HK.

    • Collaborate with Group Information Security to deploy monitoring tools safeguarding bank information.

Access governance and secure operations

  • Oversee timely completion of local and regional user access recertifications.

    • Approve and periodically review privileged access for business and IT users; recertify IT administrator and technical account access on locally and regionally managed infrastructure.

    • Serve as gatekeeper for data extraction from secure zones.

Stakeholder engagement, communications, and training

  • Build and maintain relationships with stakeholders, clients, leadership, regulators, and Head Office teams.

    • Monitor and manage security communications from Head Office and regulators.

    • Lead security awareness and training programs for employees and relevant stakeholders.

Technology, assessments, and continuous improvement

  • Support assessments for infrastructure and applications; perform periodic security reviews and audits.

    • Recommend enhancements to controls and processes based on emerging threats and best practices.

Reporting, budgeting, and resource management

  • Report timely status of projects, programs, risks, events, and incidents to the Group CISO.

    • Prepare spending proposals; manage local security budgets and resources effectively.

Skills and experience
• Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field.
• 10+ years in information security with strong knowledge of security principles, practices, and technologies.
• Proven experience designing and implementing enterprise security programs.
• Hands-on incident management and incident response expertise.
• Demonstrated leadership, including managing and influencing virtual/remote teams.
• Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills.
• Successful track record working with regulators and regulatory bodies.
• Experience in ICT Risk Management and Business Continuity.
• Industry certifications (e.g., CISSP, CISM, CRISC) preferred.


Our Values
 Accountability: Taking ownership for tasks and challenges, as well as seeking continuous improvement
 Hands-on: Being proactive to rapidly deliver high-quality results
 Passionate: Being committed and striving for excellence
 Solution-driven: Focusing on client outcomes and treating clients fairly with a risk-aware mindset
 Partnership-oriented: Promoting collaboration and teamwork. Working together with an entrepreneurial spirit.

Please ensure to attach a cover letter to your CV when filling the application.
Application
Should you wish to apply for this position use this link to apply.

jobs.title: Foundation Manager
jobs.description:
## Job Summary

The Pension Fund Manager is in charge of managing and administering the company pension fund, ensuring compliance with current regulations and protection of the interests of the employees of affiliated companies against the economic consequences of old age, death, and disability. He/she coordinates all operational, financial, and administrative activities of the pension fund, in line with the foundation regulations, policies and legal provisions.

## Tasks and Responsibilities

- Overall responsibility for the administrative management, technical-accounting and business activities of Fondazione

- Define the financial and actuarial regulations and parameters underlying the plan in order to achieve long-term financial equilibrium

- Administrative and operational management of the pension fund, including enrolment of members, management of contributions and benefits.

- Supervises the establishment of the Annual Report, the accounting and actuarial reporting to the foundation board and the supervisory bodies as well as the reporting according to IAS 19 to EFG SA

- Coordination with external bodies (actuarial consultants, auditors, supervisory authorities) and service providers (custodian banks, asset managers).

- Supervision of pension fund investments, in accordance with the investment regulations and risk criteria.

- Supervision and control of annual accounts, financial statements and documentation required by supervisory bodies.

- Supporting the Board of Foundation in defining the pension fund's strategy, ensuring the financial stability of the foundation

- Managing communications to members, including information on benefits and regulatory changes.

- Monitoring regulatory developments in the field of pensions and updating internal procedures.

- Supervises the internal control system

- Ensure constant collaboration with the human resources department of EFG SA, agreeing procedures, identifying synergies and jointly analysing common topics

- Prepare and coordinate the meetings of the foundation board and investment committee, drawing up the relevant minutes

- Actively provide pension advice to all insured persons (active employees and pensioners.

## Requirements and Qualifications

- Degree in Economics, Finance, Actuarial Science or related disciplines.

- Proven experience in the management of pension funds, provident funds or similar institutions.

- Sound knowledge of pension legislation (e.g. LOB in Switzerland or equivalent local legislation).

- Expertise in financial management and investment supervision.

- Excellent organisational, analytical and interpersonal skills.

- Advanced knowledge of IT tools for pension management and reporting.

- Managerial competences.

- Languages: Italian, English – German and French are assets

## Working Relationships

The Administration of the Foundation is composed of a small team, where collaboration between the Pension Fund Manager and all other roles of the Team is essential.

The role implies frequent interactions with Board members, external auditors, actuaries, human resources office, reinsurer, investment advisory, responsible of investment mandates, relationship manager of the Custodian Bank.

## Decision-Making & Autonomy

Autonomous and structured management of all tasks, with a global overview of the entire operations of the Foundation.

Plans and organizes activities quickly, recognizing priorities, formulating solutions appropriate to the circumstances. Communicates his views to colleagues in a timely manner, seeking to promote transparency, circulation of information within the team and process efficiency.

## Working Conditions

Work in EFG Office Lugano with frequent travel to EFG offices or to service providers (Actuary, Investment Controller, cooperating financial institutions, …) in Geneva or Zurich.

descriptionByLocale.it:
Sintesi del Lavoro Il Responsabile del Fondo Pensionistico è incaricato della gestione e dell'amministrazione del fondo pensionistico dell'azienda, garantendo il rispetto delle normative vigenti e la protezione degli interessi dei dipendenti delle società affiliate contro le conseguenze economiche dell'età avanzata, della morte e dell'invalidità. Coordina tutte le attività operative, finanziarie e amministrative del fondo pensionistico, in linea con i regolamenti della fondazione, le politiche e le disposizioni legali.

## Compiti e Responsabilità
- Responsabilità complessiva della gestione amministrativa, tecnico-contabile e operativa della Fondazione
- Definizione delle regolamentazioni e dei parametri attuariali sottostanti il piano al fine di raggiungere un equilibrio finanziario a lungo termine
- Gestione amministrativa e operativa del fondo pensionistico, inclusa l'iscrizione dei membri, la gestione delle contribuzioni e dei benefici
- Supervisione della redazione del Rapporto Annuale, della contabilità e della relazione attuariale alla board della fondazione e agli organismi di controllo, nonché del reporting secondo IAS 19 a EFG SA
- Coordinamento con enti esterni (consulenti attuariali, revisori, autorità di controllo) e fornitori di servizi (banche custodi, gestori di asset)
- Supervisione degli investimenti del fondo pensionistico, in conformità con le regolamentazioni di investimento e i criteri di rischio
- Supervisione e controllo dei conti annuali, delle dichiarazioni finanziarie e della documentazione richiesta dagli organismi di controllo
- Supporto alla Board della Fondazione nella definizione della strategia del fondo pensionistico, garantendo la stabilità finanziaria della fondazione
- Gestione delle comunicazioni ai membri, inclusa l'informazione sui benefici e le modifiche normative
- Monitoraggio dello sviluppo normativo nel campo delle pensioni e aggiornamento delle procedure interne
- Supervisione del sistema di controllo interno
- Garanzia di una costante collaborazione con il dipartimento risorse umane di EFG SA, concordando procedure, identificando sinergie e analizzando insieme temi comuni
- Preparazione e coordinamento delle riunioni della board della fondazione e del comitato di investimento, redigendo le relative minute
- Fornire attivamente consulenza pensionistica a tutti i beneficiari (dipendenti attivi e pensionati)

## Requisiti e Qualifiche
- Laurea in Economia, Finanza, Scienze Attuariali o discipline correlate
- Esperienza comprovata nella gestione di fondi pensionistici, fondi di previdenza o istituzioni simili
- Conoscenza solida della legislazione pensionistica (es. LOB in Svizzera o legislazione locale equivalente)
- Competenza nella gestione finanziaria e supervisione degli investimenti
- Eccellenti capacità organizzative, analitiche e interpersonali
- Conoscenza avanzata degli strumenti informatici per la gestione e il reporting pensionistico
- Competenze manageriali
- Lingue: Italiano, Inglese – Tedesco e Francese sono un vantaggio

## Relazioni Lavorative L'amministrazione della Fondazione è composta da un piccolo team, dove la collaborazione tra il Responsabile del Fondo Pensionistico e tutti gli altri ruoli del Team è essenziale. Il ruolo implica interazioni frequenti con i membri della Board, revisori esterni, attuari, ufficio risorse umane, riassicuratore, consulente di investimento, responsabile degli incarichi di investimento, manager di relazione della banca custode.

## Decisioni e Autonomia Gestione autonoma e strutturata di tutte le attività, con una visione globale di tutte le operazioni della Fondazione. Pianifica e organizza rapidamente le attività, riconoscendo le priorità, formulando soluzioni appropriate alle circostanze. Comunica tempestivamente le proprie opinioni ai colleghi, promuovendo trasparenza, circolazione delle informazioni all'interno del team e efficienza dei processi.

## Condizioni di Lavoro Lavoro in ufficio EFG a Lugano con frequenti viaggi verso gli uffici EFG o i fornitori di servizi (Attuario, Controllore degli Investimenti, istituti finanziari collaborativi, ...) a Ginevra o Zurigo.
descriptionByLocale.de:
Stellenüberblick Der Stiftungsleiter ist verantwortlich für die Verwaltung und Administration des Unternehmenspensionsfonds, wobei die Einhaltung der geltenden Vorschriften und der Schutz der Interessen der Mitarbeiter der verbundenen Unternehmen vor den wirtschaftlichen Folgen des hohen Alters, des Todes und der Behinderung gewährleistet werden. Er/sie koordiniert alle operativen, finanziellen und administrativen Aktivitäten des Pensionsfonds gemäß den Stiftungsregelungen, Politiken und gesetzlichen Bestimmungen.

## Aufgaben und Verantwortlichkeiten
- Gesamte Verantwortung für die administrative Verwaltung, technisch-kontabile und geschäftliche Aktivitäten der Stiftung
- Definition der finanziellen und versicherungsmathematischen Regelungen und Parameter, die den Plan unterliegen, um eine langfristige finanzielle Gleichgewicht zu erreichen
- Administrative und operative Verwaltung des Pensionsfonds, einschließlich der Registrierung der Mitglieder, der Verwaltung von Beiträgen und Leistungen
- Überwachung der Erstellung des Jahresberichts, der Buchhaltung und der versicherungsmathematischen Berichte an den Stiftungsrat und die Aufsichtsbehörden sowie der Berichte gemäß IAS 19 an EFG SA
- Koordination mit externen Stellen (versicherungsmathematische Berater, Prüfer, Aufsichtsbehörden) und Dienstleistern (Treuhänderbanken, Vermögensverwalter)
- Überwachung der Pensionsfonds-Investitionen gemäß den Investitionsregelungen und Risikokriterien
- Überwachung und Kontrolle der Jahresrechnungen, Finanzberichte und Dokumentationen, die von den Aufsichtsbehörden gefordert werden
- Unterstützung des Stiftungsrates bei der Definition der Strategie des Pensionsfonds, Sicherstellung der finanziellen Stabilität der Stiftung
- Verwaltung der Kommunikation mit den Mitgliedern, einschließlich Informationen zu Leistungen und regulatorischen Änderungen
- Überwachung der regulatorischen Entwicklungen im Bereich der Pensionsvorsorge und Aktualisierung der internen Verfahren
- Überwachung des internen Kontrollsystems
- Sicherstellung der konstanten Zusammenarbeit mit dem Personalabteilung von EFG SA, Vereinbarung von Verfahren, Identifizierung von Synergien und gemeinsame Analyse von gemeinsamen Themen
- Vorbereitung und Koordination der Sitzungen des Stiftungsrats und des Investmentkomitees, Erstellung der entsprechenden Protokolle
- Aktive Beratung in Sachen Pensionsvorsorge für alle Versicherten (aktive Mitarbeiter und Pensionäre)

## Anforderungen und Qualifikationen
- Abschluss in Wirtschaftswissenschaften, Finanzwissenschaften, Versicherungsmathematik oder verwandten Disziplinen
- Nachweisbare Erfahrung in der Verwaltung von Pensionsfonds, Vorsorgefonds oder ähnlichen Institutionen
- Solides Wissen über Pensionsgesetzgebung (z. B. LOB in der Schweiz oder vergleichbare lokale Gesetzgebung)
- Expertise in der Finanzverwaltung und Überwachung von Investitionen
- Ausgezeichnete organisatorische, analytische und zwischenmenschliche Fähigkeiten
- Fortgeschrittene Kenntnisse in IT-Tools für Pensionsverwaltung und Berichterstattung
- Führungskompetenzen
- Sprachen: Italienisch, Englisch – Deutsch und Französisch sind ein Vorteil

## Arbeitsbeziehungen Die Verwaltung der Stiftung besteht aus einem kleinen Team, in dem die Zusammenarbeit zwischen dem Stiftungsleiter und allen anderen Rollen des Teams entscheidend ist. Die Rolle beinhaltet häufige Interaktionen mit Mitgliedern des Vorstands, externen Prüfern, Versicherungsmathematikern, Personalabteilung, Rückversicherer, Investmentberater, Verantwortlichen für Investmentaufträge, Relationship Manager der Treuhänderbank.

## Entscheidungsfindung & Autonomie Autonomes und strukturiertes Management aller Aufgaben mit einem globalen Überblick über alle Operationen der Stiftung. Planung und Organisation der Aktivitäten rasch, Prioritäten erkennen, Lösungen entsprechend der Umstände formulieren. Ansichten gegenüber Kolleginnen und Kollegen rechtzeitig kommunizieren, um Transparenz, Informationsfluss innerhalb des Teams und Prozess-effizienz zu fördern.

## Arbeitsbedingungen Arbeiten im EFG-Büro in Lugano mit häufigen Reisen zu EFG-Büros oder Dienstleistern (Versicherungsmathematiker, Investmentcontroller, kooperierende Finanzinstitute, ...) in Genf oder Zürich.
descriptionByLocale.fr:
Résumé du poste Le Gestionnaire du Fonds de Pension est chargé de la gestion et de l'administration du fonds de pension de l'entreprise, en veillant à respecter les réglementations en vigueur et à protéger les intérêts des employés des sociétés affiliées contre les conséquences économiques de la vieillesse, du décès et de l'incapacité. Il/elle coordonne toutes les activités opérationnelles, financières et administratives du fonds de pension, conformément aux règlements de la fondation, aux politiques et aux dispositions légales.

## Tâches et Responsabilités
- Responsabilité globale de la gestion administrative, technique-comptable et opérationnelle de la Fondation
- Définition des règlements et paramètres actuariels sous-jacents au plan afin d'atteindre un équilibre financier à long terme
- Gestion administrative et opérationnelle du fonds de pension, y compris l'inscription des membres, la gestion des cotisations et des prestations
- Supervision de l'établissement du Rapport Annuel, de la comptabilité et du reporting actuariel au conseil de la fondation et aux organismes de contrôle, ainsi que du reporting selon IAS 19 vers EFG SA
- Coordination avec les organismes externes (conseillers actuariels, auditeurs, autorités de contrôle) et les prestataires de services (banques dépositaires, gestionnaires d'actifs)
- Supervision des investissements du fonds de pension, conformément aux règlements d'investissement et critères de risque
- Supervision et contrôle des comptes annuels, états financiers et documents requis par les organismes de contrôle
- Soutien au Conseil de la Fondation dans la définition de la stratégie du fonds de pension, en garantissant la stabilité financière de la fondation
- Gestion des communications aux membres, y compris les informations sur les prestations et les changements réglementaires
- Suivi des développements réglementaires dans le domaine des retraites et mise à jour des procédures internes
- Supervision du système de contrôle interne
- Garantir une collaboration constante avec le service des ressources humaines d'EFG SA, en définissant des procédures, en identifiant des synergies et en analysant conjointement des sujets communs
- Préparation et coordination des réunions du conseil de la fondation et du comité d'investissement, en rédigeant les procès-verbaux correspondants
- Fournir activement des conseils en matière de retraite à tous les assurés (employés actifs et retraités)

## Exigences et Qualifications
- Diplôme en Économie, Finance, Science Actuarielle ou disciplines connexes
- Expérience prouvée dans la gestion de fonds de pension, de fonds de prévoyance ou d'institutions similaires
- Bonnes connaissances de la législation sur les retraites (ex. LOB en Suisse ou législation locale équivalente)
- Expertise en gestion financière et supervision des investissements
- Excellentes compétences organisationnelles, analytiques et interpersonnelles
- Connaissances avancées des outils informatiques pour la gestion et le reporting des retraites
- Compétences managériales
- Langues : Italien, Anglais – Allemand et Français sont un atout

## Relations de Travail L'administration de la Fondation est composée d'une petite équipe, où la collaboration entre le Gestionnaire du Fonds de Pension et tous les autres rôles de l'équipe est essentielle. Le poste implique des interactions fréquentes avec les membres du Conseil, auditeurs externes, actuaires, service des ressources humaines, réassureur, conseiller en investissement, responsable des mandats d'investissement, manager de relation de la banque dépositaire.

## Prise de Décision & Autonomie Gestion autonome et structurée de toutes les tâches, avec une vue d'ensemble globale de toutes les opérations de la Fondation. Planification et organisation rapides des activités, reconnaissance des priorités, formulation de solutions adaptées aux circonstances. Communication en temps voulu des points de vue aux collègues, en visant à promouvoir la transparence, le flux d'informations au sein de l'équipe et l'efficacité des processus.

## Conditions de Travail Travail au bureau EFG à Lugano avec des déplacements fréquents vers les bureaux EFG ou les prestataires de services (Actuaire, Contrôleur des Investissements, institutions financières collaboratives, ...) à Genève ou Zurich.

He/she coordinates all operational, financial, and administrative activities of the pension fund, in line with the foundation regulations, policies and legal provisions.

sections.heading: Working Relationships
paragraphs: The Administration of the Foundation is composed of a small team, where collaboration between the Pension Fund Manager and all other roles of the Team is essential.

The role implies frequent interactions with Board members, external auditors, actuaries, human resources office, reinsurer, investment advisory, responsible of investment mandates, relationship manager of the Custodian Bank.

sections.heading: Decision-Making & Autonomy
paragraphs: Autonomous and structured management of all tasks, with a global overview of the entire operations of the Foundation.

Plans and organizes activities quickly, recognizing priorities, formulating solutions appropriate to the circumstances. Communicates his views to colleagues in a timely manner, seeking to promote transparency, circulation of information within the team and process efficiency.

sections.heading: Working Conditions
paragraphs: Work in EFG Office Lugano with frequent travel to EFG offices or to service providers (Actuary, Investment Controller, cooperating financial institutions, …) in Geneva or Zurich.

jobs.title: Internship – Business Management, Global Markets & Treasury
jobs.description:
Our Company

EFG International is a global private banking group, offering private banking and asset management services. We serve clients in over 40 locations worldwide. EFG International offers a stimulating and dynamic work environment and strives to be an employer of choice.

Our Purpose and Mission

Empowering entrepreneurial minds to create value – today and for the future.

Job Description

As part of the Business Management team within the Global Markets & Treasury division, you will gain hands-on exposure to how strategic and operational decisions are supported through data, reporting and analysis. Working closely with senior team members, you will contribute to the production of key management information and help ensure that decision-makers have timely, accurate and insightful material at their disposal.

You will be involved in a broad range of activities, from understanding and mapping processes, managing data and dashboards to preparing presentations for senior stakeholders. This role offers an excellent opportunity to develop a solid understanding of Global Markets & Treasury business and associated financial and risk metrics, and experience how a leading financial institution operates on a day-to-day basis.

Main responsibilities

- Understanding, documenting and assisting in improving processes for Global Markets & Treasury.

- Manage and maintain data sources and dashboards, ensuring information is accurate, consistent and up to date.

- Produce regular and ad hoc management reports (financials, risk metrics and KPIs) to support monitoring and decision-making.

- Prepare clear, well-structured presentations for committees, management forums and ad hoc business requests.

- Collaborate with colleagues across functions to gather input, validate data and ensure alignment of information.

Skills and experience

- Graduate student available for six months for internship (required by University) – Depending on the needs of the organisation, the internship may be extended for an additional six month.

- Strong numerical and analytical skills, with a keen attention to detail and data quality.

- Good command of Excel and PowerPoint; familiarity with data visualisation tools (e.g. dashboards) is an advantage.

- Ability to structure information clearly and present it in a concise, visually appealing way.

- Strong communication skills and willingness to interact with different stakeholders.

- Proactive, organised and able to manage multiple tasks and deadlines. Open minded, curious and eager to learn.

- Very good knowledge of written and spoken English; additional national language is an asset.

Our Values

- Accountability : Taking ownership for tasks and challenges, as well as seeking continuous improvement.

- Hands-on : Being proactive to rapidly deliver high-quality results.

- Passionate : Being committed and striving for excellence.

- Solution-driven : Focusing on client outcomes and treating clients fairly with a risk-aware mindset.

- Partnership-oriented : Promoting collaboration and teamwork. Working together with an entrepreneurial spirit..

Please ensure to attach a cover letter to your CV when filling the application.

descriptionByLocale.it:
La nostra azienda EFG International è un gruppo bancario privato globale, che offre servizi di private banking e gestione patrimoniale. Serviamo clienti in oltre 40 sedi in tutto il mondo. EFG International offre un ambiente di lavoro stimolante e dinamico e si impegna a essere un datore di lavoro di riferimento. Il nostro scopo e la nostra missione Dare potere alle menti imprenditoriali per creare valore – oggi e per il futuro. Descrizione del lavoro Come parte del team di Business Management all'interno della divisione Global Markets & Treasury, avrai l'opportunità di acquisire esperienza pratica su come le decisioni strategiche e operative siano supportate attraverso dati, reportistica e analisi. Lavorando a stretto contatto con i membri senior del team, contribuirai alla produzione di informazioni chiave per la gestione e aiuterai a garantire che i decisori abbiano a disposizione materiale tempestivo, accurato e approfondito. Sarai coinvolto in una vasta gamma di attività, dalla comprensione e mappatura dei processi, alla gestione dei dati e dei dashboard, fino alla preparazione di presentazioni per i principali stakeholder. Questo ruolo offre un'eccellente opportunità per sviluppare una solida comprensione del business di Global Markets & Treasury e delle metriche finanziarie e di rischio associate, oltre a sperimentare come opera quotidianamente una delle principali istituzioni finanziarie. Principali responsabilità
- Comprendere, documentare e assistere nel miglioramento dei processi per Global Markets & Treasury.
- Gestire e mantenere fonti di dati e dashboard, garantendo che le informazioni siano accurate, coerenti e aggiornate.
- Produrre report di gestione regolari e ad hoc (dati finanziari, metriche di rischio e KPI) per supportare il monitoraggio e il processo decisionale.
- Preparare presentazioni chiare e ben strutturate per comitati, forum di gestione e richieste aziendali ad hoc.
- Collaborare con colleghi di diverse funzioni per raccogliere input, validare dati e garantire l'allineamento delle informazioni. Competenze ed esperienza
- Studente laureando disponibile per sei mesi di stage (richiesto dall'Università) – A seconda delle esigenze dell'organizzazione, lo stage può essere esteso per ulteriori sei mesi.
- Forti competenze numeriche e analitiche, con grande attenzione ai dettagli e alla qualità dei dati.
- Buona padronanza di Excel e PowerPoint; familiarità con strumenti di visualizzazione dei dati (ad esempio dashboard) è un vantaggio.
- Capacità di strutturare le informazioni in modo chiaro e presentarle in modo conciso e visivamente accattivante.
- Forti capacità comunicative e disponibilità a interagire con diversi stakeholder.
- Proattività, organizzazione e capacità di gestire più compiti e scadenze. Mente aperta, curiosità e voglia di imparare.
- Ottima conoscenza dell'inglese scritto e parlato; una lingua nazionale aggiuntiva è un vantaggio. I nostri valori
- Responsabilità: Assumersi la responsabilità dei compiti e delle sfide, cercando continuamente di migliorare.
- Praticità: Essere proattivi per fornire rapidamente risultati di alta qualità.
- Passione: Essere impegnati e puntare all'eccellenza.
- Orientamento alle soluzioni: Concentrarsi sui risultati per i clienti e trattarli in modo equo con una mentalità consapevole del rischio.
- Orientamento alla partnership: Promuovere la collaborazione e il lavoro di squadra. Lavorare insieme con uno spirito imprenditoriale. Si prega di allegare una lettera di presentazione al CV al momento della candidatura.
descriptionByLocale.de:
Unser Unternehmen EFG International ist eine globale Privatbankengruppe, die Dienstleistungen im Bereich Private Banking und Vermögensverwaltung anbietet. Wir betreuen Kunden an über 40 Standorten weltweit. EFG International bietet ein anregendes und dynamisches Arbeitsumfeld und strebt danach, ein bevorzugter Arbeitgeber zu sein. Unser Zweck und unsere Mission Unternehmerische Köpfe zu befähigen, Wert zu schaffen – heute und in der Zukunft. Stellenbeschreibung Als Teil des Business Management Teams innerhalb der Global Markets & Treasury Division erhalten Sie praktische Einblicke, wie strategische und operative Entscheidungen durch Daten, Berichterstattung und Analysen unterstützt werden. In enger Zusammenarbeit mit erfahrenen Teammitgliedern tragen Sie zur Erstellung von wichtigen Managementinformationen bei und helfen sicherzustellen, dass Entscheidungsträger rechtzeitige, genaue und aufschlussreiche Materialien zur Verfügung haben. Sie werden in eine breite Palette von Aktivitäten eingebunden, von der Analyse und Dokumentation von Prozessen über die Verwaltung von Daten und Dashboards bis hin zur Erstellung von Präsentationen für wichtige Stakeholder. Diese Rolle bietet eine hervorragende Gelegenheit, ein solides Verständnis für das Geschäft von Global Markets & Treasury sowie für die damit verbundenen finanziellen und risikobezogenen Kennzahlen zu entwickeln und zu erleben, wie ein führendes Finanzinstitut im Tagesgeschäft arbeitet. Hauptaufgaben
- Verstehen, Dokumentieren und Unterstützen bei der Verbesserung von Prozessen für Global Markets & Treasury.
- Verwalten und Pflegen von Datenquellen und Dashboards, um sicherzustellen, dass Informationen genau, konsistent und aktuell sind.
- Erstellung regelmäßiger und ad-hoc Managementberichte (Finanzen, Risikokennzahlen und KPIs) zur Unterstützung von Überwachung und Entscheidungsfindung.
- Vorbereitung klarer, gut strukturierter Präsentationen für Ausschüsse, Managementforen und ad-hoc Geschäftsanforderungen.
- Zusammenarbeit mit Kollegen aus verschiedenen Funktionen, um Input zu sammeln, Daten zu validieren und die Abstimmung von Informationen sicherzustellen. Fähigkeiten und Erfahrungen
- Hochschulabsolvent, der für ein sechsmonatiges Praktikum verfügbar ist (erforderlich von der Universität) – Je nach den Bedürfnissen der Organisation kann das Praktikum um weitere sechs Monate verlängert werden.
- Starke numerische und analytische Fähigkeiten mit einem ausgeprägten Auge für Details und Datenqualität.
- Gute Kenntnisse in Excel und PowerPoint; Vertrautheit mit Datenvisualisierungstools (z. B. Dashboards) ist von Vorteil.
- Fähigkeit, Informationen klar zu strukturieren und prägnant sowie visuell ansprechend zu präsentieren.
- Starke Kommunikationsfähigkeiten und Bereitschaft, mit verschiedenen Stakeholdern zu interagieren.
- Proaktiv, organisiert und in der Lage, mehrere Aufgaben und Fristen zu verwalten. Offen, neugierig und lernbereit.
- Sehr gute Kenntnisse in schriftlichem und gesprochenem Englisch; eine zusätzliche Landessprache ist von Vorteil. Unsere Werte
- Verantwortlichkeit: Übernahme von Verantwortung für Aufgaben und Herausforderungen sowie kontinuierliche Verbesserung anstreben.
- Praktisch: Proaktiv sein, um schnell qualitativ hochwertige Ergebnisse zu liefern.
- Leidenschaftlich: Engagiert sein und nach Exzellenz streben.
- Lösungsorientiert: Fokus auf Kundenergebnisse und faire Behandlung der Kunden mit einem risikobewussten Ansatz.
- Partnerschaftsorientiert: Förderung von Zusammenarbeit und Teamarbeit. Zusammenarbeit mit einem unternehmerischen Geist. Bitte fügen Sie ein Anschreiben Ihrem Lebenslauf bei, wenn Sie sich bewerben.
descriptionByLocale.fr:
Notre entreprise EFG International est un groupe bancaire privé mondial, offrant des services de banque privée et de gestion d'actifs. Nous servons des clients dans plus de 40 emplacements à travers le monde. EFG International offre un environnement de travail stimulant et dynamique et s'efforce d'être un employeur de choix. Notre objectif et notre mission Donner aux esprits entrepreneuriaux les moyens de créer de la valeur – aujourd'hui et pour l'avenir. Description du poste En tant que membre de l'équipe de gestion d'entreprise au sein de la division Global Markets & Treasury, vous aurez une exposition pratique à la manière dont les décisions stratégiques et opérationnelles sont soutenues par des données, des rapports et des analyses. En travaillant en étroite collaboration avec des membres seniors de l'équipe, vous contribuerez à la production d'informations clés pour la gestion et aiderez à garantir que les décideurs disposent de matériel opportun, précis et pertinent. Vous serez impliqué dans un large éventail d'activités, allant de la compréhension et de la cartographie des processus, à la gestion des données et des tableaux de bord, en passant par la préparation de présentations pour les parties prenantes seniors. Ce rôle offre une excellente opportunité de développer une compréhension solide des activités de Global Markets & Treasury ainsi que des indicateurs financiers et de risque associés, et de découvrir le fonctionnement quotidien d'une institution financière de premier plan. Principales responsabilités
- Comprendre, documenter et aider à améliorer les processus pour Global Markets & Treasury.
- Gérer et maintenir les sources de données et les tableaux de bord, en veillant à ce que les informations soient exactes, cohérentes et à jour.
- Produire des rapports de gestion réguliers et ad hoc (financiers, indicateurs de risque et KPI) pour soutenir le suivi et la prise de décision.
- Préparer des présentations claires et bien structurées pour les comités, les forums de gestion et les demandes commerciales ad hoc.
- Collaborer avec des collègues de différentes fonctions pour recueillir des contributions, valider les données et garantir l'alignement des informations. Compétences et expérience
- Étudiant diplômé disponible pour un stage de six mois (exigé par l'université) – Selon les besoins de l'organisation, le stage peut être prolongé de six mois supplémentaires.
- Solides compétences en calcul et en analyse, avec une grande attention aux détails et à la qualité des données.
- Bonne maîtrise d'Excel et de PowerPoint ; la familiarité avec les outils de visualisation de données (par exemple, tableaux de bord) est un atout.
- Capacité à structurer clairement les informations et à les présenter de manière concise et visuellement attrayante.
- Excellentes compétences en communication et volonté d'interagir avec différents intervenants.
- Proactif, organisé et capable de gérer plusieurs tâches et échéances. Ouvert d'esprit, curieux et désireux d'apprendre.
- Très bonne maîtrise de l'anglais écrit et parlé ; une langue nationale supplémentaire est un atout. Nos valeurs
- Responsabilité : Assumer la responsabilité des tâches et des défis, tout en cherchant à s'améliorer en permanence.
- Pratique : Être proactif pour livrer rapidement des résultats de haute qualité.
- Passionné : Être engagé et viser l'excellence.
- Orienté solutions : Se concentrer sur les résultats pour les clients et les traiter équitablement avec une approche consciente des risques.
- Orienté partenariat : Promouvoir la collaboration et le travail d'équipe. Travailler ensemble avec un esprit entrepreneurial. Veuillez joindre une lettre de motivation à votre CV lors de votre candidature.

summary:
Our Company

EFG International is a global private banking group, offering private banking and asset management services. We serve clients in over 40 locations worldwide. EFG International offers a stimulating and dynamic work environment and strives to be an employer of choice.

EFG is committed to providing an equitable and inclusive working environment that is founded on the principle of mutual respect. Joining our team means experiencing a supportive environment, where your contributions are valued and recognised. We strongly believe that the diversity of our teams gives us a competitive advantage by fostering better decision-making and greater innovation.

Our Purpose and Mission

Empowering entrepreneurial minds to create value – today and for the future.

We are a private bank, offering personalised solutions on a global scale to private and institutional clients. Our sustainable success is based on our talents and on how we partner with our clients and communities to create lasting value.
